instruction_en: How do I know if my soil is good for farming?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na …õkyer…õ s…õ m'asase y…õ y…õ papa ma okuafo adwuma?
response_en: Good farming soil is dark, crumbly, and has a pleasant earthy smell. Push your finger 5cm into the soil ‚Äî if it holds shape but breaks apart easily, it is good. You can also send a sample to an agricultural office for a proper soil test.
response_tw: Asase pa w…î okuafo adwuma mu y…õ …õda, na …õpepee yie, na …õw…î asase hua. Fa wo nsateaa wura asase no mu s…õntimita 5 ‚Äî s…õ …õkura ne ho nanso …õpae yie a, …õy…õ asase pa. Wobetumi de asase bi k…î okuafo ofisi s…õ w…îhw…õ so yie.

instruction_en: What is the best fertilizer for maize?
instruction_tw: Ferefere b…õn na …õy…õ papa ma aburo?
response_en: For maize, use NPK fertilizer (15-15-15) at planting, then top-dress with urea about 4-6 weeks after planting. Apply 2 bags of NPK per acre at planting and 1 bag of urea per acre when top-dressing.
response_tw: Aburo ho, fa NPK ferefere (15-15-15) de to asase mu bere a wode aburow to mu no. Afei fa urea gu so s…õnt…î 4-6 akyi a wode aburow to mu no. Fa NPK b…ît…î 2 gu acre baako biara mu bere a wode aburow no gu fam, na fa urea b…ît…î 1 gu acre baako biara mu bere a wode to so.

instruction_en: Can I use animal manure instead of chemical fertilizer?
instruction_tw: Metumi de mmoa d…îte…õ adi dwuma mmom sen nnuru ferefere?
response_en: Yes, animal manure from cows, goats, or chickens is an excellent organic fertilizer. Apply well-composted manure 2-3 weeks before planting. It improves soil structure and provides slow-release nutrients.
response_tw: Aane, mmoa aguare fi boo, abirekyi, anaa akr…îma y…õ ferefere papa pa ara. De aguare a …õawie wo yie no to asase so wiemu 2-3 ansa na wobede aba to mu. ∆êsiesie asase mu na …õma aduan ho nhwiren ba bebree.

instruction_en: How do I make compost at home?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mey…õ compost w…î fie?
response_en: Mix green materials like grass and vegetable waste with brown materials like dry leaves and straw in a pile. Keep it moist and turn it every 2 weeks. After 2-3 months, you will have rich compost ready for your farm.
response_tw: Fa Nne…õma a …õy…õ ahabammono te s…õ sare ne nnuaba funu fra nne…õma a …õy…õ bruu te s…õ nhaban a …õwo ne sarew. Ma …õnny…õ nwini na dane no dap…õn abien biara. Abosome 2-3 akyi, wob…õnya compost pa a …õawie wo yie w…î wo afuo ho.

instruction_en: How do I improve sandy soil that dries out quickly?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi y…õ asinti…õ asase a …õwo nt…õm no yiye?
response_en: Add organic matter like compost, animal manure, or crop residues to sandy soil. This helps retain moisture and nutrients. Mulching the soil surface also reduces water evaporation. Consider drip irrigation for better water management.
response_tw: De nhwiren foforo te s…õ compost, mmoa aguare, anaa nnuaba ho nsennua ka asinti…õ asase no ho. Eyi boa ma nsu ne nnuaba mu nnuane nka mu yie. S…õ wode mulch kata asase no so a, …õma nsuo nwo nt…õm. Fa nsuo gu fam nkakrankakra (drip irrigation) di dwuma de hw…õ nsuo so yiye.

instruction_en: How do I prevent soil erosion on my farm?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mema asase amma …õnhuru w…î m'afuo mu?
response_en: Plant cover crops or grass along slopes, practice contour farming, build terraces on steep land, and mulch bare soil. Avoid leaving soil uncovered during heavy rain seasons.
response_tw: To nnuaba anaa at…õnt…õnni w…î kwantempon ho, y…õ contour farming, si terrace w…î asase a …õkwan so, na de mulch s…õ…õ asase a …õda. B…î hw…õ asase da ho aky…õ w…î nsuo tenten bere mu.

instruction_en: My soil is too acidic. What can I do?
instruction_tw: M'asase y…õ acidic dodo. D…õn na metumi y…õ?
response_en: Apply agricultural lime (calcium carbonate) to raise the pH of acidic soil. Typically apply 1-2 tonnes per hectare. Apply lime at least 3-4 weeks before planting and mix it well into the soil.
response_tw: De agricultural lime (calcium carbonate) to asase acidic no so s…õ …õma pH no sor. Taa de tonne 1-2 to hectare biara so. De lime to asase so wiemu 3-4 ansa na wode aba to mu na ka ho yie w…î asase mu.

instruction_en: What is the ideal soil pH for growing vegetables?
instruction_tw: Asase pH b…õn na …õy…õ …îkor…î ma nnuan?
response_en: Most vegetables grow best in soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0. Below 6.0 is too acidic and above 7.5 is too alkaline. You can test your soil pH using a simple pH testing kit from an agro-input shop or send a sample to your nearest agricultural laboratory.
response_tw: Nnuan bebree da sor pa w…î asase a pH y…õ 6.0 ne 7.0 ntam. ∆êsua 6.0 a …õy…õ acidic dodo na …õboro 7.5 a …õy…õ alkaline dodo. Wobetumi hw…õ wo asase pH w…î pH testing kit fi agro-input ade…õt…î h…î anaa de asase bi k…î agricultural laboratory a …õb…õn wo.

instruction_en: What is green manure and how do I use it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na nhwiren-tew ferefere y…õ na …õde…õn na mefa di dwuma?
response_en: Green manure means growing specific crops like legumes or grasses and then plowing them back into the soil while still green. This adds organic matter and nitrogen to the soil. Common green manure crops include cowpea, mucuna, and lablab. Plow them in at least 3 weeks before planting your main crop.
response_tw: Nhwiren-tew ferefere kyer…õ kura nnuaba bi te s…õ ab…îdwe…õ anaa at…õnt…õnni na afei boro w…în gu asase mu bere a w…îda koraa. Eyi de nhwiren foforo ne nitrogen ka asase ho. Nhwiren-tew ferefere nnuaba a w…îtaa di dwuma ne ab…îdwe…õ, mucuna, ne lablab. Boro w…în gu asase mu wiemu 3 ansa na wokura wo nnuaba titiriw.

instruction_en: How do I know if my fertilizer is working?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menim s…õ m'ferefere y…õ adwuma?
response_en: Signs that fertilizer is working include: darker green leaves, faster plant growth, larger leaves, and higher yield compared to unfertilized plots. If plants remain yellowish and grow slowly after 2-3 weeks of fertilizer application, the fertilizer may not have been applied correctly or the soil may have other problems.
response_tw: Ahohyenam s…õ ferefere y…õ adwuma ne: nhaban a w…îy…õ tuntum-akyen pa ara, nnuaba a w…îda sor nt…õm, nhaban k…õse, ne nnuaba a w…îda sor dodo sen beae a w…îanto ferefere. S…õ nnuaba da hoho na w…îda sor br…õ akyi a ferefere to so wiemu 2-3 a, ferefere no be…õ to so yie anaa asase no w…î ns…õm fofor…î.

instruction_en: Can over-fertilizing damage my crops?
instruction_tw: Ferefere dodo tumi s…õe m'nnuaba anaa?
response_en: Yes, over-fertilizing can burn plant roots and leaves, cause excessive leafy growth with poor fruiting, pollute water sources, and waste money. Always follow the recommended rates on fertilizer bags or advice from your extension officer. Start with less and increase gradually based on crop response.
response_tw: Aane, ferefere dodo tumi hyew nnuaba ntini ne nhaban, ma nnuaba nhaban da sor dodo na aba ba sua, ha nsuo mu, na seaa sika. Bue ferefere bag so krataa nhy…õso anaa fa wo extension officer ns…õm di. Fi ferefere sua na fa dodo so kakra kakra w…î nnuaba tebea so.

instruction_en: What does NPK mean on a fertilizer bag?
instruction_tw: D…õn na NPK kyer…õ w…î ferefere bag so?
response_en: NPK stands for Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K) ‚Äî the three main nutrients plants need. Nitrogen helps leafy growth, Phosphorus supports root development and flowering, and Potassium strengthens stems and improves fruit quality. The numbers like 15-15-15 show the percentage of each nutrient in the bag.
response_tw: NPK kyer…õ Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), ne Potassium (K) ‚Äî aduan titiriw mmi…õnsa a nnuaba hia. Nitrogen boa nhaban da sor, Phosphorus boa ntini ne abode, na Potassium ma tibo y…õ den na aba y…õ pa. N…îma te s…õ 15-15-15 no kyer…õ k…õse a aduan biara w…î bag mu.

instruction_en: When should I not apply fertilizer to my crops?
instruction_tw: Bere b…õn na ns…õ meto ferefere?
response_en: Do not apply fertilizer when: heavy rain is forecast as it will wash away the fertilizer, the soil is very dry as it can burn roots, plants are severely stressed from pests or disease, soil is waterlogged, or crops are very close to harvest. Always apply when soil has some moisture and weather is stable.
response_tw: Mto ferefere bere a: osu tenten reb…õba efis…õ …õb…õhoro ferefere no k…î, asase yera pa ara efis…õ betumi hyew ntini, nnuaba hia adwummakers anaa yade…õ k…õse, nsuo hy…õ asase mu, anaa nnuaba reb…õwie ase nt…õm. To ferefere bere a asase w…î nsuo kakra na …õwim tebea y…õ p…õ.

instruction_en: How do I test my soil at home without a kit?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mehw…õ m'asase w…î fie a menw…î kit?
response_en: Simple home tests: for pH, mix soil with vinegar ‚Äî if it fizzes the soil is alkaline. Mix soil with baking soda and water ‚Äî if it fizzes the soil is acidic. For texture, squeeze moist soil ‚Äî clay soil holds shape, sandy soil crumbles, and loam soil holds shape then crumbles. For organic matter, dark soil has more organic matter than pale soil.
response_tw: Fie hw…õ anamm…în ketewa: pH ho, ka asase ne vinegar ‚Äî s…õ …õp…î a asase no y…õ alkaline. Ka asase ne baking soda ne nsuo ‚Äî s…õ …õp…î a asase no y…õ acidic. Nsusuw ho, hia asase nte ‚Äî amane asase kura ne ho, asinti…õ asase pae, na loam asase kura ne ho na afei pae. Nhwiren foforo ho, asase …õda w…î nhwiren foforo dodo sen asase hoho.

instruction_en: Why does my soil become hard and how do I fix it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na …õma asase y…õ den na …õhia yiye?
response_en: Soil becomes hard from: overuse of heavy machinery which compacts the soil, lack of organic matter, repeatedly plowing at the same depth creating a hardpan layer, frequent flooding followed by drying, and low biological activity. To fix it, add organic matter, practice deep tillage occasionally, and use cover crops.
response_tw: Asase y…õ den fi: egyapade k…õse ho di dwuma dodo a …õhia asase, nhwiren foforo aduan ho hia, bere biara boro asase sukyer…õ koro so a …õy…õ hardpan, nsuo tenten a …õto akyi na asase yera, ne nkwa a …õdi asase mu hia. S…õ wohwehw…õ siesie no a, de nhwiren foforo ka ho, bere bi boro asase mu tenten, na kura asase-bo nnuabas.

instruction_en: When is the best time to plant maize in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Bere b…õn na …õy…õ …îkor…î s…õ wode aburow to mu w…î Ghana?
response_en: In Ghana, the best time to plant maize is at the beginning of the rainy season ‚Äî March to April in the south, and May to June in the north. Plant when you have received at least 2-3 good rains so the soil is well moistened.
response_tw: W…î Ghana, bere pa ara s…õ wode aburow to mu ne nsuo bere mfiase ‚Äî ∆êb…î kwan so Oforisuo w…î apuei fam, na Ay…õwohomumo kwan so Mumu w…î atifi fam. To aba mu bere a wanya nsu mm…õn 2-3 yie na asase no atenante yie.

instruction_en: How deep should I plant maize seeds?
instruction_tw: ∆êsukyer…õ b…õn na mede aburow aba to asase mu?
response_en: Plant maize seeds 3-5 cm deep. If the soil is dry, plant slightly deeper to reach moisture. Space seeds 25-30 cm apart within rows and 75-90 cm between rows.
response_tw: De aburow aba to asase mu s…õntimita 3-5. S…õ asase no yera a, to mu kakra dodo na wo nsa adu nsukuro ho. Gyae asukyer…õ 25-30 s…õntimita w…î aba biara ntam na 75-90 s…õntimita w…î nnua so ntam.

instruction_en: What is crop rotation and why is it important?
instruction_tw: Dea …õy…õ s…õ wosesa nnuaba gu asase mu na ad…õn na …õy…õ papa?
response_en: Crop rotation means growing different crops on the same land each season. For example, grow maize in season 1, then cowpea in season 2. This breaks pest cycles, improves soil fertility, and increases yields.
response_tw: Nnuaba sesae kyer…õ s…õ wokura ahorow nnuaba w…î asase koro so bere biara. Nhw…õso so, kura aburow bere 1 mu, afei kura ab…îdwe…õ w…î bere 2 mu. Eyi bubu adwummakers ntam, y…õ asase mu aduan ho nhwiren na …õma nnuaba da sor dodo.

instruction_en: How do I select good seeds for planting?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mepaw aba pa s…õ mede to mu?
response_en: Choose certified seeds from reputable agro-input dealers. Good seeds are uniform in size, free from damage, and have a high germination rate of at least 85%. Avoid using seeds from the previous harvest for hybrid varieties.
response_tw: Paw aba a w…îakyer…õw won fi agro-input at…îfo a w…în din ay…õ pa. Aba pa y…õ p…õ w…î bo…î mu, w…î mfomso, na w…în nyini y…õ k…õse sen 85%. B…î hw…õ de aba a wogye fi d…õm nnuaba meto aba hybrid aba no ase.

instruction_en: How do I prepare the land before planting?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mesiesie asase aba to mu ansa?
response_en: Clear the land of weeds and old crop residues. Plow or dig to loosen the soil to about 20-30 cm deep. Break up large clods and remove stones. Level the surface and apply basal fertilizer or compost before planting.
response_tw: Yi sare ne nnua funu fi asase no so. Twa anaa hwe asase no mu k…î s…õntimita 20- 30 mu. Bubu asase ak…õse no na yi abo. Siesie fam no yiye na fa basal ferefere anaa compost gu so ansa na wode aba no agu fam.

instruction_en: Can I grow vegetables in the dry season?
instruction_tw: Metumi adua nn…îbae w…î …îp…õ bere mu?
response_en: Yes, you can grow vegetables in the dry season with irrigation. Crops like tomatoes, pepper, onions, leafy vegetables, and cucumbers grow well in dry season with proper watering.
response_tw: Aane, wobetumi kura nnuan w…î …îp…õ bere mu s…õ wode nsuo gu so. Nn…îbae te s…õ ntomate, mako, gyeene, nhaban nn…îbae, ne cucumber nyini yiye w…î …îp…õ bere mu s…õ wode nsuo gu so yiye a.

instruction_en: What is the best way to transplant seedlings without killing them?
instruction_tw: Kwan b…õn na …õy…õ papa a y…õfa so si nnua nketewa baabi foforo a …õrenwu?
response_en: Water seedlings well the day before transplanting. Transplant in the late afternoon or on a cloudy day to reduce heat stress. Dig the hole bigger than the root ball. Remove seedling from container carefully without disturbing roots. Firm soil around roots and water immediately. Provide shade for 2-3 days if needed.
response_tw: Gu nnua nketewa no nsuo pii da a …õdi kan ansa na wosi w…în baabi foforo. Si w…în baabi foforo anwummere anaa da a ewim y…õ nwunu na ama owia nhyew w…în. Tu amoa a …õs…õ k…õse sen ntini no. Yi nnua nketewa no fi nkuku mu br…õoo a wunhaw ntini no. Fa asase kata ntini no ho yiye na gu nsuo nt…õm ara. Fa sunsuma br…õ w…în nna 2-3 s…õ …õhia2.

instruction_en: How do I know if my seeds have germinated well?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menim s…õ m'aba apue yie?
response_en: Check germination 5-10 days after planting depending on the crop. Good germination means at least 80% of planted seeds have sprouted uniformly. If germination is poor, check if seeds were planted too deep, soil was too dry or waterlogged, temperature was too extreme, or seeds were old. Replant gaps with fresh seeds immediately.
response_tw: Hw…õ s…õ aba no afifi nna 5-10 akyi a wode agu fam5. S…õ aba no fifi yiye a, …õkyer…õ s…õ aba a wode agu fam no 80% p…õ afifi p…õp…õ…õp…õ80. S…õ aba no mfifi yiye a, hw…õ s…õ wode agu fam dodo, asase no y…õ kusuu anaa nsuo d…î…îso w…î mu, ewim y…õ hye anaa nwunu dodo, anaa aba no dedaw. Fa aba foforo gu mmeae a …õda h…î no nt…õm ara.

instruction_en: What is a nursery and when should I use one?
instruction_tw: Nursery y…õ d…õn na bere b…õn na …õs…õ s…õ y…õde di dwuma?
response_en: A nursery is a protected area where seeds are germinated and young plants are raised before transplanting to the main field. Use a nursery for: small expensive seeds like tomato, pepper, and onion; crops that need a head start before rains; and crops where seedling survival in the field is uncertain. Nurseries save seeds and improve stand establishment.
response_tw: Nursery y…õ beae a w…îb…î ho ban a w…îde aba gu mu na w…îtete nnua nketewa ansa na w…îde ak…îdua w…î afuo k…õse no mu. Fa nursery di dwuma ma: aba nketewa a ne bo…î y…õ den te s…õ ntomate, mako, ne gyeene; nn…îbae a …õhia s…õ wofi ase nt…õm ansa na osuo aba; ne nn…îbae a …õny…õ awiegye s…õ …õb…õtena ase w…î afuo no mu. Nursery boa ma aba no y…õ yiye na …õma nnua no nyini yiye.

instruction_en: Can I plant different vegetables together in one plot?
instruction_tw: Metumi adua nn…îbae ahorow pii w…î afuo koro mu anaa?
response_en: Yes, mixing vegetables is called companion planting and can be very beneficial. Good combinations include tomatoes with basil (basil repels tomato pests), maize with beans and squash (the Three Sisters method), onions with carrots (they repel each other's pests), and marigolds with any vegetable (repels many insects). Avoid planting crops from the same family together as they share pests.
response_tw: Aane, nn…îbae a wudua no bom y…õ companion planting na …õboa pii. Nnuaba a w…îy…õ adwuma yie w…î bom ne ntomato ne basil (basil kora ntomato adwummakers ase), aburow ne ab…îdwe…õ ne squash (Three Sisters anamm…în), gyene ne carrot (w…îkora w…în ho adwummakers ase), na marigold ne nnuan biara (…õkora adwummakers bebree ase). Hw…õ yiye na woannua nn…îbae a …õw…î abusua koro mu w…î beae koro efis…õ w…îtaa nya yare koro.

instruction_en: How do I plant crops on slopes to prevent water runoff?
instruction_tw: Kwan b…õn na …õs…õ s…õ mefa so dua nn…îbae w…î mmep…îw so na ama nsuo anntu k…î?
response_en: On slopes, always plant across the slope (contour planting) rather than up and down. Make ridges along contour lines to hold water. Plant cover crops or grasses on the steepest parts. Use tied ridges (ridges with cross-ties every few meters) to capture rainwater. Mulch heavily to protect soil between plants.
response_tw: W…î kwantempon so, to aba twa kwantempon so daa (contour planting) na mmom k…î sor na gu ase. Y…õ ntwaho w…î contour lines no ho na ama nsuo akora. To asase-bo nnuabas anaa at…õnt…õnni w…î beae a …õkwan pa ara. Fa tied ridges (ntwaho a w…îne ky…õ …õt…î so baako biara) s…õ …õgye osu nsuo. De mulch to so papaapa s…õ …õkora asase nnuaba ntam.

instruction_en: When is the right time to transplant tomato seedlings to the field?
instruction_tw: Mfe…õ b…õn na …õfata s…õ mesi ntomato nnuaba mfoforo ananmu?
response_en: Transplant tomato seedlings when they are 4-6 weeks old, have 4-6 true leaves, are 15-20 cm tall, and have a sturdy stem. Seedlings that are too young may not survive transplanting shock. Seedlings that are too old (over 8 weeks) become root-bound and may not establish well in the field.
response_tw: Si ntomato nnuaba mfoforo ananmu bere a w…îdin nnawotwe 4-6, w…îw…î nhaban 4-6, w…în soro…î y…õ s…õntimita 15-20, na w…îw…î tibo den. Nnuaba mfoforo a w…îkum dodo betumi anky…õ si ananmu no …îk…îm. Nnuaba mfoforo a w…îadin dodo (nnawotwe 8 kenkan) y…õ root-bound na betumi ansi yie w…î afuo mu.

instruction_en: What is raised bed farming and what are its benefits?
instruction_tw: D…õn na asase-sor afuo farming y…õ na mfaaso b…õn na …õw…î?
response_en: Raised bed farming means growing crops on elevated soil mounds or frames above the ground level. Benefits include better drainage, improved soil structure, easier weeding and harvesting, higher yield per square meter, better pest management, and ability to farm in poor soil areas. It is ideal for small spaces and home gardens.
response_tw: Asase-sor afuo farming kyer…õ kura nnuaba w…î asase mounts anaa frame a …õda sor sen asase soronko. Mfaaso ho ne: nsuo pue mu yie, asase mu siesie pa, nhaban tia ne nnuaba gye y…õ mmer…õ, nnuaba da sor dodo w…î square meter biara mu, adwummakers hw…õ ase yie, na tumi kura w…î asase a enni pa mu. ∆êfata afuo ketewa ne fie afuo.

instruction_en: How do I grow crops in containers or pots when I have no land?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mekura nnuaba w…î nkuku anaa pots mu s…õ menni asase?
response_en: Growing in containers is called container gardening. Use large buckets, old tires, plastic bags, or purpose-made pots. Fill with a mix of soil, compost, and sand for good drainage. Good container crops include tomatoes, peppers, leafy vegetables, herbs, and onions. Place in a sunny spot and water frequently as containers dry out faster than ground soil.
response_tw: Kura w…î nkuku mu s…õ container gardening. Fa bucket k…õse, tay…õ kedee, plastik bags, anaa nkuku a w…îy…õ w…în ho. Hye asase, compost, ne asinti…õ bom mu s…õ nsuo pue mu yie. Nnuaba a w…îda sor yie w…î nkuku mu ne ntomato, mako, nhaban nnuan, nduaba aduan, ne gyene. Si w…în w…î beae a owia hye na de nsuo to p…õ efis…õ nkuku yera nt…õm sen asase.

instruction_en: My maize leaves have black holes and something is eating them. What do I do?
instruction_tw: M'aburow nhaban w…î tumtum na w…îredidi won. D…õn na mey…õ?
response_en: This is likely the Fall Armyworm. Look inside the whorl for worms. Apply insecticides like emamectin benzoate early morning or late evening. Sand mixed with ash applied into the whorl also helps as an organic option.
response_tw: Eyi tumi y…õ Abor…înoma Adwummaker K…õse adwummaker. Hw…õ nhaban mu s…õ w…îhwehw…õ adwummakers anaa w…î ho ntama. De adwummakers dawuro te s…õ emamectin benzoate guare owia da ano anaa nn…înhwerew mu. Asinti…õ a waka nkamfo ka ho no tumi de gu nhaban mu s…õ nhwiren dawuro.

instruction_en: How do I control weevils in my stored grain?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi de weevils hy…õ ase w…î m'aduan a maguina mu?
response_en: Dry the grain properly before storage to below 13% moisture. Use hermetic airtight bags which suffocate insects. You can also mix grain with diatomite dust or apply approved grain protectant chemicals.
response_tw: Yaw aduan no yie ansa na waguina mu na …õsua 13% nsuku. Fa hermetic bags a w…îapo so yie a …õkum adwummakers w…î mu. Wobetumi de aduan ka diatomite tukutuku ho anaa de aduan adwummakers dawuro a w…îagyei no to so.

instruction_en: My tomato plants are wilting and waterlogged. What is wrong?
instruction_tw: M'ntomato nnua rewo na mede nsu hy…õ won. D…õn na …õhia?
response_en: Wilting despite watering could be bacterial wilt or fusarium wilt ‚Äî soil-borne diseases. Cut the stem ‚Äî if the inside is brown or oozes liquid, it is wilt disease. Remove infected plants and rotate crops for 2-3 years.
response_tw: Nwonwon a …õba w…î nsuo a wode hy…õ won akyi no betumi y…õ bacteria nwiinwii yade…õ anaa fusarium yade…õ wilt ‚Äî yade…õ a …õfiri asase mu. Twa tibo no ‚Äî s…õ mu y…õ …õda anaa nsuo pue a, …õy…õ wilt yade…õ. Yi nnua a w…îayar…õ no fi afuo mu na sesa nnuaba mfe 2-3.

instruction_en: My cassava leaves have turned yellow and are curling. What is wrong?
instruction_tw: M'bankye nhaban redan sika na rekyekye. D…õn na …õhia?
response_en: This could be Cassava Mosaic Disease spread by whiteflies. Symptoms include yellowing and leaf curling. There is no cure ‚Äî remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Plant resistant varieties and control whitefly populations.
response_tw: Eyi betumi y…õ Cassava Mosaic Yade…õ a fitaa abowa adwummakers de br…õ. Ahohyenam ho ne nhaban hoho ne kyekyere. Enni dawuro ‚Äî yi nnua a w…îayar…õ no na seaa won nt…õm ara. To aba a …õtumi hy…õ ase na kora fitaa abowa adwummakers ase.

instruction_en: What is the Fall Armyworm and what damage does it cause?
instruction_tw: D…õn na Abor…înoma Adwummaker K…õse ne …õy…õ …îk…îmm b…õn?
response_en: Fall Armyworm is a highly destructive caterpillar that mainly attacks maize. It can destroy an entire field within days. Control with insecticides like emamectin benzoate, apply early morning, and target the whorls where larvae hide.
response_tw: Abor…înoma Adwummaker K…õse y…õ abor…înoma adwummaker a …îs…õe dodo na …îtaa k…î aburow so. ∆Ütumi seaa afuo koro nyinaa w…î nnafoa mu. Kora ase w…î adwummakers dawuro te s…õ emamectin benzoate, de to owia da ano, na hy…õ nhaban mu a adwummakers no k…îfa ho.

instruction_en: How do I control aphids on my crops?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi kora aphid ase w…î m'nnuaba so?
response_en: Aphids are small soft insects that cluster on young shoots. Spray them off with a strong jet of water, or apply neem oil, insecticidal soap, or systemic insecticides like imidacloprid. Introduce natural predators like ladybirds if possible.
response_tw: Aphid y…õ adwummakers ketewa a w…îhyia w…î nnuaba mfoforo so. De nsuo k…õse guare won afi, anaa de neem dawuro, adwummakers dawuro sab…î, anaa adwummakers dawuro te s…õ imidacloprid gu won so. De abor…îkyir adwummakers a …îdi w…în te s…õ ladybird fa ba s…õ wonya tumi.

instruction_en: What causes black spots on my tomato fruits?
instruction_tw: D…õn na …õma m'ntomato aba so w…î ntuntum nsono?
response_en: Dark spots at the bottom of tomatoes are Blossom End Rot caused by calcium deficiency and irregular watering. Maintain consistent watering and apply calcium-rich fertilizers. Dark spots on the sides may be from fungal diseases.
response_tw: Ntuntum nsono a …õw…î ntomato aba ase no y…õ Blossom End Rot, a calcium hia na nsuo to ns…õ ama. Kora nsuo to p…õ na de calcium ferefere to so. Ntuntum nsono a …õw…î af√£ so betumi fi fungal yade…õ.

instruction_en: My groundnut leaves have spots. What disease is this?
instruction_tw: M'nkatie nhaban w…î nsono. Yade…õ b…õn na …õy…õ?
response_en: Leaf spots on groundnuts are usually Early Leaf Spot or Late Leaf Spot fungal diseases. Spray with fungicides like mancozeb or chlorothalonil every 2 weeks. Plant resistant varieties and practice crop rotation.
response_tw: Nhaban nsono w…î nkatie so taa y…õ Early Nhaban Nsono Yade…õ anaa Late Nhaban Nsono Yade…õ fungal yade…õ. De mancozeb anaa chlorothalonil guare mmere 2 biara. To aba a …õtumi hy…õ ase na y…õ nnuaba sesae.

instruction_en: How do I keep pests out of my vegetable garden?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mema adwummakers amma w…înba m'nnuan afuo mu?
response_en: Practice crop rotation, remove crop residues after harvest, use pest-resistant varieties, plant trap crops like marigolds, use nets or row covers, and monitor your farm regularly.
response_tw: Y…õ nnuaba sesae, yi nnuaba nsennua akyi a wogye, fa aba a …õtumi adwummakers ase, to trap crops te s…õ marigold s…õ …õtwe adwummakers fi nnuaba pa ho, fa nets anaa row covers, na hw…õ wo afuo so p…õ.

instruction_en: How do I control grasshoppers on my farm?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi kora totrobonini ase w…î m'afuo so?
response_en: Control grasshoppers by early morning spraying with approved insecticides like lambda-cyhalothrin. Biological control using Metarhizium fungi is also effective. Community-wide spraying is more effective than individual farm treatment.
response_tw: Wobetumi kora totrobonini ase w…î owia da ano guare adwummakers dawuro a w…îagyei te s…õ lambda-cyhalothrin. Metarhizium fungi biological control nso y…õ adwuma pa. Oman nyinaa guare y…õ adwuma pa sen afuo baako biara.

instruction_en: There are white insects on my vegetables. What are they and how do I remove them?
instruction_tw: Adwummakers fitaa a w…îw…î m'nnuan so y…õ d…õn na metumi yi w…în?
response_en: Whiteflies are tiny white insects that suck plant sap and spread viruses. Control them by spraying insecticidal soap, neem oil, or imidacloprid. Yellow sticky traps also help. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizer which attracts them.
response_tw: Fitaa abowa y…õ adwummakers fitaa ketewa a w…înom nnuaba mogya na w…îde yade…õ br…õ. Kora w…în ase w…î adwummakers dawuro sab…î, neem dawuro, anaa imidacloprid so. Yellow sticky traps nso boa. B…î hw…õ nitrogen ferefere dodo efis…õ …õtwe w…în.

instruction_en: Birds are eating my rice and millet. How do I protect my crops?
instruction_tw: Anomaa redidi m'…ît…õ ne …ît…õ pa. ∆êde…õn na mekora m'nnuaba?
response_en: Use bird scaring methods: move scarecrows regularly to remain effective, hang reflective tape or old CDs around the field, use noise-makers, and recruit children to chase birds during grain-filling stage. Growing early-maturing varieties reduces the bird damage period.
response_tw: Fa anomaa kora anamm…în: tware anomaa kyer…õma p…õ s…õ …õy…õ adwuma yie, si reflective tape anaa CD kedee w…î afuo ho, fa nne ade…õ, na fa mmofra boa s…õ w…îtaa anomaa no bere a aba no repuo. Kura aba a …õwie nt…õm maa anomaa s…õe bere sua.

instruction_en: How do I control mango mealybug?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi kora mangoro mealybug ase?
response_en: Mango mealybugs appear as white cottony masses on stems and fruits. Control by pruning heavily infested branches, applying insecticidal soap or neem oil, banding tree trunks with sticky tape to prevent crawlers from climbing, and introducing natural predators like Cryptolaemus beetles.
response_tw: Mangoro mealybug ba s…õ dodow fitaa w…î nnua tiboo ne aba so. Kora w…în ase w…î twa ntini a w…îhyia so k…õse, de adwummakers dawuro sab…î anaa neem dawuro gu so, de sticky tape kaa ntini ho, na de Cryptolaemus beetle a …îdi w…în fa ba.

instruction_en: My cowpea has rust disease. What do I do?
instruction_tw: M'ab…îdwe…õ w…î rust yade…õ. ∆êde…õn na mey…õ?
response_en: Bean rust appears as orange-brown powdery spots on leaves. Remove and destroy severely infected leaves. Spray with fungicides containing mancozeb or sulfur. Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead watering which spreads spores.
response_tw: Bean rust ba s…õ nsono …õda-b…î…î w…î nhaban so. Yi na seaa nhaban a w…îayar…õ k…õse. De mancozeb anaa sulfur fungicide guare. To aba a …õtumi hy…õ ase. B…î hw…õ nsuo guare firi sor efis…õ …õde yade…õ tete.

instruction_en: Striga weed is destroying my rice. What can I do?
instruction_tw: Striga nhaban foforo res…õe m'…ît…õ. D…õn na metumi y…õ?
response_en: Striga is a parasitic weed that attaches to crop roots. Control includes using Striga-resistant varieties, applying Imazapyr-coated seeds, hand pulling Striga before it flowers, growing trap crops like Sudan grass, and applying fertilizer to help crops outcompete Striga.
response_tw: Striga y…õ nhaban foforo a …îkyer…õ ne ho w…î nnuaba ntini so. Kora ase w…î de aba a …õtumi Striga ase hy…õ, de Imazapyr-coated aba, twe Striga w…î nsa ansa na …îba abode, kura trap crops te s…õ Sudan grass, na de ferefere to s…õ …õboa nnuaba no nko …îkan.

instruction_en: How do I use pesticides safely and effectively on my farm?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mefa adwummakers dawuro di dwuma a …õho tumi w…î m'afuo mu?
response_en: Always read the label before use. Wear protective clothing including gloves, boots, mask, and long sleeves. Mix pesticides in open air away from water sources. Never eat, drink, or smoke while handling pesticides. Store in original containers away from children.
response_tw: Kenkan krataa a …õw…î so ansa na wodi dwuma. Hye ntoma a …õkora wo ho te s…õ nsatea nhoma, mbot…î, aboa, ne ntama tenten. Ka dawuro no w…î baabi a mframa w…î h…î tete fi nsuo ho. Mmu aduan, nnom nsu, anaa mfa sigaret bere a wode dawuro no di dwuma.

instruction_en: What is aflatoxin and what does it do to my crops?
instruction_tw: D…õn na aflatoxin dze y…õ na …õy…õ m'nnuaba d…õn?
response_en: Aflatoxin is a toxic substance produced by molds that grow on grains, groundnuts, and spices when stored in humid conditions. It is harmful to humans and animals and causes liver disease. Prevent it by drying crops properly and using clean airtight storage.
response_tw: Aflatoxin Dze y…õ ns…õm a …õy…õ …îk…îm a fungus a …õda sor w…î nnuaba, nkatie, ne nduaba so bere a w…îaguina w…î huhu mu na …õba. ∆êy…õ onipa ne mmoa …îk…îm na …õma afu yade…õ. Kora ase w…î yaw nnuaba yie ansa na waguina na de nkuku a apo so yie guina.

instruction_en: What is Integrated Pest Management (IPM)?
instruction_tw: D…õn na adwummakers hw…õ anamm…în kaa bom (IPM) y…õ?
response_en: Integrated Pest Management (IPM) is an approach that combines multiple pest control methods to minimize chemical use. It includes: monitoring crops regularly to catch problems early, using resistant varieties, practicing good agronomic practices, using biological controls like beneficial insects, and only using chemicals as a last resort when pest populations exceed economic thresholds.
response_tw: Adwummakers Hw…õ Anamm…în Kaa Bom (IPM) y…õ anamm…în a w…îka adwummakers kora anamm…în bebree bom s…õ chemical fa sua. Mu w…î: hw…õ nnuaba so p…õ s…õ wuhu ns…õm nt…õm, fa aba a …õtumi hy…õ ase, y…õ okuafo adwuma pa, fa biological controls te s…õ adwummakers a w…îboa, na fa chemicals p…õ bere a adwummakers a …õy…õ dodo sene sika mu a wob…õnya.

instruction_en: How do I make a homemade organic pesticide from pepper?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mey…õ fie nhwiren adwummakers dawuro fi mako?
response_en: Blend 10-15 hot chili peppers with 1 litre of water. Strain through cloth, add a little liquid soap, and dilute with 5 more litres of water. Spray on affected plants in the morning or evening. The capsaicin in chili repels and kills soft-bodied insects like aphids and mites. Reapply every 3-4 days and after rain.
response_tw: Blend mako hye 10-15 ne nsuo litre 1 bom. Hun w…î ntama mu, de liquid soap kakra ka ho, na fa nsuo litre 5 bio ka ho. Guare nnuaba a w…îayar…õ so owia da ano anaa anwummere. Capsaicin a …õw…î mako mu kora adwummakers a w…în ho y…õ mmer…õ te s…õ aphid ne mite ase. To so bio nnafoa 3-4 biara na osu akyi.

instruction_en: What are nematodes and what do they do to my crops?
instruction_tw: D…õn na nematode y…õ na …õy…õ m'nnuaba d…õn?
response_en: Nematodes are microscopic worms that live in soil. Some are harmful to crops ‚Äî they attack roots causing galls (swellings), stunted growth, and yellowing. They are common in sandy soils and warm climates. Control by rotating crops, using resistant varieties, adding organic matter to soil, and solarizing soil (covering with clear plastic to heat it and kill nematodes during the dry season).
response_tw: Nematode y…õ nwir…õntwi a w…îte asase mu a w…îhunu da. Bi y…õ nnuaba …îk…îm ‚Äî w…îtwe ntini ho ma …õy…õ k…õse, nnuaba nni sor yie, na nhaban hoho. W…îtaa w…î asinti…õ asase ne ahuhuro mu. Kora ase w…î sesa nnuaba, fa aba a …õtumi hy…õ ase, de nhwiren foforo ka asase ho, na solarize asase (fa plastik fitaa s…õ…õ so s…õ …õhye mu na kum nematode w…î …îp…õ bere mu).

instruction_en: What is powdery mildew and how do I control it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na tukutuku fitaa yade…õ y…õ na …õde…õn na metumi kora ase?
response_en: Powdery mildew appears as white powdery patches on leaves and stems. It is caused by fungi and thrives in warm, dry conditions with high humidity. It weakens plants and reduces yield. Control by: removing infected leaves, improving air circulation by pruning, spraying with diluted baking soda solution (1 tablespoon per litre of water), neem oil, or sulfur-based fungicides.
response_tw: Tukutuku fitaa yade…õ ba s…õ fitaa tukutuku a …õw…î nhaban ne tibo so. Fungus ama na …õda sor pa w…î ahuhuro mu a …õw…î huhu. ∆êma nnuaba y…õ mmer…õ na eba nnuaba a w…îda sor sua. Kora ase w…î: yi nhaban a w…îayar…õ, ma mframa pue mu yie w…î twa nnua so, guare baking soda a w…îasusu mu (tablespoon baako w…î nsuo litre biara mu), neem dawuro, anaa sulfur-based fungicide.

instruction_en: My plant leaves look burnt and yellow. What is wrong?
instruction_tw: M'nnuaba nhaban …îky…õ y…õ s…õ w…îahyew. D…õn na …õhia?
response_en: Leaf tip burn can be caused by: fertilizer burn from too much nitrogen or potassium, salt stress from poor quality irrigation water, calcium deficiency, water stress during hot weather, or wind damage. Check your fertilizer rates and water quality first. Apply calcium fertilizer and ensure consistent watering. The condition often improves once the cause is corrected.
response_tw: Nhaban …îky…õ hyew betumi fi: ferefere burn fi nitrogen anaa potassium dodo, salt …îk…îm fi nsuo a enni pa, calcium hia, nsuo hia w…î ahuhuro mu, anaa mframa …îk…îm. Hw…õ wo ferefere k…õse ne nsuo ho pa ansa. De calcium ferefere to so na kora nsuo to p…õ. Tebea no taa y…õ pa bere a wuy…õ dawuro.

instruction_en: How do I identify spider mites and control them?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mehunu spider mite na metumi kora w…în ase?
response_en: Spider mites are tiny red or brown creatures barely visible to the naked eye. Signs include: fine webbing on leaves and stems, tiny yellow or white dots on leaves (feeding damage), and leaves turning bronze or yellow. Control by spraying with strong water jet, neem oil, insecticidal soap, or miticides. Mites thrive in hot and dry conditions ‚Äî adequate watering helps prevent them.
response_tw: Spider mite y…õ adwummakers …õda-k…îk…î…î anaa …õda-b…î…î ketewa a wohunu w…în yie. Ahohyenam ho ne: nwir…õntwi a …õw…î nhaban ne tibo so, nsono hoho anaa fitaa ketewa ketewa w…î nhaban so (adidi akyinnye), na nhaban a w…îredan k…îk…î…î anaa hoho. Kora ase w…î de nsuo k…õse guare, neem dawuro, adwummakers dawuro sab…î, anaa miticide. Mite da sor pa w…î ahuhuro ne …îp…õ bere ‚Äî nsuo pa boa s…õ w…înba.

instruction_en: What causes root rot and how do I prevent it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na …õma ntini s…õe na …õde…õn na metumi kora ase?
response_en: Root rot is caused by soil-borne fungi, mainly when soil stays waterlogged for too long. It causes roots to turn brown and mushy, plants to wilt even when soil is wet, and eventual plant death. Prevent by: improving soil drainage, avoiding overwatering, not planting in low-lying waterlogged areas, using raised beds, treating seeds with fungicide before planting, and rotating crops.
response_tw: Ntini s…õe fi asase fungus, titiriw bere a nsuo da asase mu tenten dodo. ∆êma ntini dan …õda ne mmer…õ, nnuaba nwo bere a asase nte, na nnua wu. Kora ase w…î: siesie asase nsuo pue anamm…în, b…î hw…õ nsuo to dodo, mto nnuaba w…î asase ase a nsuo hia mu, fa asase-sor afuo, de aba ka fungicide ho ansa na woto mu, na sesa nnuaba.

instruction_en: How do I control thrips on my onions?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi kora thrips ase w…î m'gyene so?
response_en: Thrips are tiny insects that feed on onion leaves causing silvery streaks and distorted growth. Control by: removing heavily infested leaves, spraying with neem oil or insecticides like spinosad or imidacloprid, mulching to prevent thrips from pupating in soil, avoiding planting near other infested crops, and using blue sticky traps for monitoring.
response_tw: Thrips y…õ adwummakers ketewa a w…îdi gyene nhaban so ma …õkyer…õ silver ne nnuaba a w…înni sor yie. Kora ase w…î: yi nhaban a w…îhyia so k…õse, guare neem dawuro anaa adwummakers dawuro te s…õ spinosad anaa imidacloprid, de mulch to s…õ thrips ansi asase mu, b…î hw…õ to nnuaba b…õn nnuaba fofor…î a w…îahyia so, na fa blue sticky traps hw…õ w…în so.

instruction_en: What is damping-off disease in seedlings?
instruction_tw: D…õn na nnuaba-mfoforo wu yade…õ yade…õ y…õ w…î nnuaba mfoforo mu?
response_en: Damping off is a fungal disease that causes seedlings to suddenly collapse and die, usually at the soil level where the stem meets the ground. The stem appears water-soaked and constricted. It is caused by overwatering, poor drainage, overcrowded seedlings, and unsterilized potting mix. Prevent by using sterilized soil, avoiding overwatering, ensuring good air circulation, and treating seeds with fungicide.
response_tw: Nnuaba-mfoforo wu yade…õ y…õ fungal yade…õ a …õma nnuaba mfoforo tu nt…õm na w…îwu, titiriw w…î asase so a tibo ne asase hyia h…î. Tibo no y…õ s…õ nsuo da mu na …õhyia. Nsuo dodo, nsuo pue anamm…în a enni pa, nnuaba mfoforo a w…îhyia so, ne potting mix a wonsi amu ama. Kora ase w…î fa asase a w…îasi amu, b…î hw…õ nsuo dodo, kora mframa pue mu yie, na de aba ka fungicide ho ansa na woto.

instruction_en: How do I know if my crops are nutrient deficient?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mehunu s…õ m'nnuaba hia aduan?
response_en: Common deficiency signs: Nitrogen deficiency ‚Äî older leaves turn yellow starting from the bottom of the plant. Iron deficiency ‚Äî young leaves turn yellow but veins stay green. Phosphorus deficiency ‚Äî leaves turn purple or reddish. Potassium deficiency ‚Äî leaf edges turn brown and crispy. Calcium deficiency ‚Äî new leaves distort and curl. A soil test will confirm which nutrients are lacking.
response_tw: Aduan hia ahohyenam a w…îtaa hu: Nitrogen hia ‚Äî nhaban kedee a w…îw…î nnua ase hoho fi nnua ase. Iron hia ‚Äî nhaban foforo hoho nanso nhwiren ho da tuntum-akyen. Phosphorus hia ‚Äî nhaban dan purpura anaa k…îk…î…î. Potassium hia ‚Äî nhaban ho dan …õda ne den. Calcium hia ‚Äî nhaban foforo pae ne kyekye. Asase hw…õ b…õkyer…õ aduan a ehia.

instruction_en: How do I control the tomato leaf miner?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi kora ntomato nhaban miner ase?
response_en: Tomato leaf miners are larvae that tunnel inside leaves creating winding white trails. Control by: removing and destroying heavily mined leaves, applying spinosad or abamectin insecticides, using yellow sticky traps to catch adult flies, releasing parasitic wasps as biological control, and avoiding planting tomatoes after tomatoes in the same location.
response_tw: Ntomato nhaban miner y…õ larvae a w…îk…î nhaban mu mu na w…îy…õ kwan fitaa a ahwimhwim. Kora ase w…î: yi ne seaa nhaban a w…îahyia so k…õse, de spinosad anaa abamectin guare, fa yellow sticky traps gye sor…î a …õy…õ hogua mu no, de parasitic wasp guare s…õ biological control, na b…î hw…õ to ntomato akyi ntomato w…î faako koro.

instruction_en: What is citrus greening disease and how can it be managed?
instruction_tw: D…õn na citrus greening yade…õ y…õ na wobetumi ay…õ dawuro?
response_en: Citrus greening (also called Huanglongbing or HLB) is a deadly bacterial disease spread by the Asian citrus psyllid insect. Symptoms include yellowing leaves, misshapen bitter fruits, and tree decline. There is currently no cure ‚Äî infected trees should be removed and destroyed to prevent spread. Control psyllid insects and use certified disease-free planting material.
response_tw: Citrus greening (a w…îfre no Huanglongbing anaa HLB) y…õ bacterial yade…õ a …õkum na Asian citrus psyllid adwummaker de tete. Ahohyenam ho ne nhaban hoho, aba a as…õe ne a w…în d…õm y…õ den, na nnua hia. Enni dawuro saa ara ‚Äî yi nnua a w…îayar…õ na seaa w…în s…õ yade…õ anntete. Kora psyllid adwummakers ase na fa aba a w…îakyer…õw w…î ho to mu.

instruction_en: How much water do my crops need?
instruction_tw: Nsuo b…õn na m'nnuaba hia?
response_en: Most crops need about 25-50mm of water per week. Signs of water stress include wilting and dry soil. In the dry season, water deeply but less frequently to encourage deep rooting. Early morning watering reduces evaporation.
response_tw: Nnuaba bebree hia nsuo s…õntimita 25-50 dap…õn biara mu. Nsuo hia ahohyenam ho ne nwonwon ne asase a …õyera. ∆Üp…õ bere mu, de nsuo to asase mu k…õse nanso mmere pii bi no de nsuo to ho s…õ nnua no na w…îde w…în ntini k…î ase yie. Owia da ano nsuo guare mma nsukyer…õ y…õ dodo.

instruction_en: How do I know when my crops need water?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menim bere a m'nnuaba hia nsuo?
response_en: Push your finger 5cm deep into the soil ‚Äî if dry, water is needed. Watch for early morning wilting as a sign of drought stress. Sandy soils need watering more often than clay soils. Tomatoes and vegetables need more frequent watering than cassava.
response_tw: Hia wo nsateaa asase mu s…õntimita 5 ‚Äî s…õ …õyera a, hia nsuo. Hw…õ owia da ano nwonwon s…õ …õkyer…õ nsuo hia. Asinti…õ asase hia nsuo pii sen amane asase. Ntomato ne nnuan hia nsuo pii sen bankye.

instruction_en: What is the best irrigation method for a small farm?
instruction_tw: Nsuo to anamm…în b…õn na …õy…õ …îkor…î w…î afuo ketewa mu?
response_en: For small farms, drip irrigation is the most water-efficient method. Sprinkler irrigation is good for larger areas. Watering cans or buckets work for very small plots.
response_tw: Afuo ketewa ho, nsuo tete-tete ky…õ y…õ anamm…în a …õfa nsuo nky…õn pa ara. Sprinkler irrigation y…õ …îkor…î w…î beae k…õse. Nsuo ky…õ anaa bucket y…õ adwuma w…î afuo ketewa ketewa.

instruction_en: Can I use borehole water to irrigate my crops?
instruction_tw: Metumi de borehole nsuo to nnuaba so?
response_en: Yes, borehole water is generally good for irrigation. However some borehole water can be salty or have high mineral content which can damage crops over time. If possible, test the water at an agricultural lab.
response_tw: Aane, borehole nsuo y…õ …îkor…î s…õ wode nsuo gu so. Nanso borehole nsuo bi tumi y…õ …õwa anaa minerals w…î mu dodo a …õtumi s…õe nnuaba bere akyi. S…õ wonya tumi a, hw…õ nsuo no w…î agricultural lab.

instruction_en: How do I know when my maize is ready to harvest?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menim s…õ m'aburow awie ase na …õy…õ a megye no?
response_en: Maize is ready when the husks turn brown and dry, the silks are dark brown/black, and the kernels are hard. This is usually 90-120 days after planting depending on the variety.
response_tw: Aburow awie ase bere a ntwaho no dan …õda na ayera, nhwiren no adan …õda k…îk…î…î/tuntum, na aba no ay…õ den. Eyi taa ba nnafoa 90-120 a wode aba to mu no akyi w…î aba no ahodo…î so.

instruction_en: What is the best time of day to harvest crops?
instruction_tw: Bere b…õn w…î da mu na …õy…õ …îkor…î s…õ wogye nnuan?
response_en: Harvest vegetables in the early morning when temperatures are cool. This helps produce stay fresh longer. Avoid harvesting in midday heat. For leafy vegetables, harvest with dew still present for crispness.
response_tw: Gye nnuan owia da ano bere a owia hye no ay…õ tirim. Eyi ma nnuan no tena p…õ sen ara. B…î hw…õ w…î awia mu ahuhuro mu gye nnuan. Nhaban nnuan ho, gye won a nsuyiri da won so ara da no ho nkae.

instruction_en: When should I harvest my tomatoes?
instruction_tw: Bere b…õn na megye m'ntomato?
response_en: Harvest tomatoes when they are fully colored but still firm. For selling to distant markets, harvest at the breaker stage when they begin to change color so they ripen during transport without getting damaged.
response_tw: Gye ntomato bere a w…îanya w…în rere na w…îda den ara. S…õ wohwehw…õ t…în w…î akura a …õw…î akyiri a, gye w…în bere a w…îredan w…în rere na w…îreb…õware w…î kwan so a w…îde k…î efis…õ wontumi ntwam.

instruction_en: How do I harvest my cassava properly?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na megye m'bankye yie?
response_en: Cassava is usually harvested 9-18 months after planting. Cut the stem at 30-40 cm height then loosen the soil around the plant with a hoe and pull up the roots. Handle roots gently to avoid bruising.
response_tw: Bankye taa gye w…î abosome 9-18 akyi a wode to mu. Twa tibo no w…î s…õntimita 30-40 so, afei de ak…îde fa asase a …õhyia nnua no ho na twe ntini no sor. De ntini no p…õ efis…õ s…õ ehow a …õma …õs…õe nt…õm.

instruction_en: How do I harvest groundnuts when some pods are still developing?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na megye nkatie a aba bi nko?
response_en: Harvest groundnuts when leaves start yellowing. Loosen the soil with a hoe first then pull the whole plant upward. Shake off excess soil and thresh on a clean tarpaulin. Dry pods in the sun for 3-5 days before storage.
response_tw: Gye nkatie bere a nhaban no redan sika. Fa ak…îde fa asase no ho ansa, afei twe nnua no nyinaa sor. Woso asase a aka no afi na bu w…î tarpaulin horo so. Yaw aba no w…î owia mu nnafoa 3-5 ansa na waguina.

instruction_en: How do I harvest oil palm fruits properly?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na megye abe aba yie?
response_en: Palm fruits are ready when they turn reddish-orange and some fruits start falling naturally from the bunch. Use a long-handled chisel or harvesting sickle to cut the bunch from the tree. Collect fallen fruits from the ground too. Process within 24-48 hours of harvest to avoid free fatty acid buildup which reduces oil quality.
response_tw: Abe aba wie ase bere a w…îdan k…îk…î…î-…õda na aba bi firi b…î afi ne ho p…õ. Fa long-handled chisel anaa harvesting sickle twa b…î no fi dua no ho. Gye aba a atu gu asase so nso. Y…õ adwuma w…î dafe 24-48 akyi a wogye s…õ free fatty acid nbi dodo a …õsua ngo ho pa.

instruction_en: When is a mango ripe and ready to harvest?
instruction_tw: Bere b…õn na mangoro awie ase s…õ megye?
response_en: Mangoes are ready to harvest when: the fruit changes color from green to yellow, orange, or red depending on variety; the fruit flesh near the seed turns yellow; the fruit gives slightly when gently pressed; and the fruit smells sweet near the stem. For commercial harvest, pick slightly before full ripeness so they ripen during transport.
response_tw: Mangoro awie ase bere a: aba no resen rere fi akyen k…î hoho, …õda, anaa k…îk…î…î w…î aba no ahodo…î so; aba no nnompe a …õb…õn aba no dan hoho; aba no tumi pii kakra bere a wode fa so p…õ; na aba no nhwiren foforo pue fi tibo ho. T…în ho, gye ansa na …õawi p…õp…õ…õp…õ na …õware w…î kwan so.

instruction_en: How do I harvest pepper without damaging the plant?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na megye mako a …õns…õe nnua no?
response_en: Harvest pepper using scissors or a sharp knife to cut the fruit with a small piece of stem ‚Äî never pull the pepper off by hand as it damages the plant. Harvest when fruits are fully developed and have changed to their final color. Regular harvesting every 3-5 days encourages the plant to produce more fruits throughout the season.
response_tw: Gye mako fa scissors anaa sekan tew twa aba no a tibo kakra ka ho ‚Äî mfa wo nsa twe mako afi nnua no ho da efis…õ …õs…õe nnua no. Gye bere a aba no awi yie na w…îadan w…în rere. Gye p…õ mmere 3-5 biara ma nnua no s…õ …õk…î ma aba dodo w…î bere mu nyinaa.

instruction_en: How do I harvest and process coconut?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na megye ne y…õ adwuma w…î nkuto aba?
response_en: Collect fallen shea fruits from the ground during the harvest season (May to August). Remove the sweet pulp by soaking and washing. Dry the nuts for 1-2 weeks in the sun. Crack the dried shells to get the kernels. Dry kernels further before storing or processing into shea butter. Never dry shea nuts on bare soil or over fire as this reduces butter quality.
response_tw: Gye nkuto aba a atu gu asase so w…î gye bere mu (∆Üb…õnem kwan so ∆Üsannaa). Yi asew a …õda mu w…î da w…î nsuo mu na hohoro. Yaw aba no owia mu nnawotwe 1-2. Bubu nkuku a ayaw no s…õ wunya aba no mu ade. Yaw aba mu ade no bio ansa na waguina anaa y…õ nkuto ngo. Mfa nkuto aba yaw w…î asase firi so anaa ogya so da efis…õ …õsua ngo ho pa.

instruction_en: How do I know when my pineapple is ready to harvest?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menim s…õ m'abor…îfo awie ase s…õ megye?
response_en: A pineapple is ready when: the skin changes from green to yellow starting at the base, the fruit gives a dull hollow sound when tapped, the fruit smells sweet and fragrant, the leaves at the top pull out easily, and the fruit is firm but gives slightly when pressed. Pineapples do not ripen further after harvest, so timing is critical.
response_tw: Abor…îfo awie ase bere a: foforo no resen fi akyen k…î hoho fi ase, aba no su s…õ k…îk…î bere a wode b…î so, aba no nhwiren foforo pue na …õy…õ d…õ, nhaban a …õw…î so no twe ba yie, na aba no y…õ den nanso tumi pii kakra. Abor…îfo nware bio akyi a wogye, enti bere y…õ titiriw pa ara.

instruction_en: How do I store my maize to prevent aflatoxin contamination?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na meguina m'aburow na aflatoxin dze nka ho?
response_en: Dry maize properly to below 13% moisture before storage. Store in clean, dry, well-ventilated structures. Use hermetic bags to prevent insect infestation. Avoid mixing good grain with damaged grain.
response_tw: Yaw aburow yie na …õsua 13% nsuku ansa na waguina. Guina w…î fie a …õhoro, yera, na …õw…î mframa pa. Fa hermetic bags s…õ adwummakers nni mu. B…î hw…õ ka aburo pa ne aburo a …õas…õe no bom.

instruction_en: How long can I store cassava after harvesting it?
instruction_tw: Bere ahe na metumi guina bankye a megye no akyi?
response_en: Fresh cassava deteriorates within 24-72 hours after harvest. For longer storage, process into dried chips, gari, or flour. Waxing the roots can extend fresh storage to about a week. Keep in cool, shaded areas.
response_tw: Bankye foforo s…õe w…î dafe 24-72 akyi a wogye no. S…õ wohwehw…õ guina bere tenten a, y…õ no bankye chips a ayaw, gari, anaa mfurow. Waxing bankye no de tumi ma no tena p…õ bere koro nnawotwe mu. Wura no w…î bae a …õhuru na owia ntra mu.

instruction_en: How do I store yam properly after harvest?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na meguina m'bayer…õ yie?
response_en: Store yams in a cool, dark, well-ventilated area. Traditional yam barns work well. Avoid contact with soil and keep yams off the ground. Inspect regularly and remove any rotting tubers. Yams can last 4-6 months.
response_tw: Guina bayer…õ w…î bae a …õhuru, …õsum na mframa pue mu yie. Amammer…õ bayer…õ guina sukuu y…õ adwuma pa. B…î hw…õ asase nka w…în na ma w…în na w…întena asase so. Hw…õ w…în so p…õ na yi …îbayer…õ a …õres…õe. Bayer…õ tumi tena yie abosome 4-6.

instruction_en: How do I protect my stored food from rats and insects?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mekora m'aduan a maguina fi …îkra ne okusie ho?
response_en: Keep the storage area clean and free of food scraps. Seal all holes and cracks in walls. Use snap traps or approved rodenticides. Store bags on raised platforms away from walls. Cats are also effective.
response_tw: Kora guina fie no horo na aduan asennua ntena mu. Popa tumtum ne …õw…îw…î nyinaa w…î b…î mu. Fa snap traps anaa dawuro a w…îagyei de kum …îkra. Guina bags w…î platform a …õda sor tete akyi. Agyinamoa nso y…õ adwuma pa.

instruction_en: How do I store tomatoes without a fridge?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na meguina m'ntomato a menhy…õ w…în fridge mu?
response_en: Store tomatoes at room temperature away from direct sunlight. Do not stack heavily. Keep them stem-side down. Tomatoes last 1-2 weeks this way. For longer preservation, dry, can, or make tomato paste. Never refrigerate unripe tomatoes.
response_tw: Guina ntomato w…î fie mu osu a …õy…õ p…õ s…õ owia ntra w…în so t…ît…î. Mfa w…în si so dodo. De w…în si w…în tibo fam. Ntomato tumi tena nnawotwe 1-2 eyi ho. S…õ wohwehw…õ tena bere tenten a, yaw, kanta, anaa y…õ ntomato paste.

instruction_en: How do I use hermetic storage on a small budget?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mey…õ hermetic guina a …õbo…î y…õ ketewa?
response_en: You can make low-cost hermetic storage using: triple-layer plastic bags where you place grain in an inner plastic bag, seal it, place in a second bag, seal it, then place in a third outer bag and seal. Or use metal drums with tight-fitting lids sealed with tape. Store off the ground on pallets. This method is very effective for 6-12 months of storage.
response_tw: Wobetumi y…õ hermetic guina a …õbo…î y…õ ketewa w…î: plastik bags mmi…õnsa a wode aduan si plastik bag mu, po so, de si bag abien mu, po so, afei de si bag mmi…õnsa mu na po so. Anoa fa metal drums a w…în soro y…õ den a w…îde tape po so. Guina w…î pallet so afi asase so. Anamm…în no y…õ adwuma yie pa ara w…î guina abosome 6-12 mu.

instruction_en: How do I preserve fresh fish or meat on the farm without a fridge?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na meguina nsuomnam anaa nam a ayaw w…î afuo so a menw…î fridge?
response_en: Dry fish or meat thoroughly until it is very hard and dry. Wrap tightly in cloth or paper, then in a plastic bag. Store in a cool, dry, ventilated area away from moisture and insects. You can smoke the meat or fish to extend shelf life. Inspect weekly and re-dry immediately if any moisture is detected. Properly dried fish or meat can last 2-3 months.
response_tw: Yaw nsuomnam anaa nam no yie kosi s…õ …õy…õ den na yera. De ntama anaa krataa s…õ…õ mu yie, na afei de plastik bag s…õ…õ. Guina w…î bae a …õhuru, yera, na mframa pue mu fi nsuo ne adwummakers ho. Wobetumi bu nam anaa nsuomnam no ewi s…õ …õma no tena yie. Hw…õ so nnawotwe biara na yaw nt…õm s…õ nsuo w…î mu. Nam anaa nsuomnam a ayaw yie tena abosome 2-3.

instruction_en: How do I prevent mould from growing in my stored produce?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mema fungus amma …õnda sor w…î m'aduan a maguina?
response_en: Mold grows when moisture is present. Prevent it by: drying produce thoroughly before storage, storing in well-ventilated areas, using absorbent packaging like cloth or paper not plastic, checking stored goods regularly, removing any moldy produce immediately to prevent spread, keeping storage areas clean, and never mixing new produce with old.
response_tw: Fungus da sor bere a nsuo w…î h…î. Kora ase w…î: yaw aduan no yie ansa na waguina, guina w…î beae a mframa pue mu yie, fa packaging a …õsuo nsuo te s…õ ntama anaa krataa na mfa plastik, hw…õ aduan a waguina p…õ, yi aduan a fungus ato ho nt…õm s…õ anntete, kora guina beae no horo, na mmfa aduan foforo ka aduan kedee bom da.

instruction_en: What is hermetic silo storage and can a small farmer use it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na ntwene-nkuma guina y…õ na okuafo ketewa tumi nya ase anaa?
response_en: Cold storage is refrigerated storage that keeps produce at low temperatures to extend shelf life. Small farmers can access it through: farmer cooperatives that own shared cold storage facilities, government-run cold storage centers, private cold storage services that charge per crate or kilogram, mobile cold storage trucks, and evaporative coolers which are low-cost non-electrical cooling structures.
response_tw: Ntwene-nkuma guina y…õ aduan guina a w…îde ntwene nkuma so ma aduan tena yie bere tenten. Okuafo ketewa tumi nya ase w…î: okuafo cooperative a w…îw…î ntwene-nkuma guina bom, …îman ntwene-nkuma guina centres, onipa h…î ntwene-nkuma guina nnwuma a w…îsua crate anaa kilogram biara, mobile ntwene-nkuma guina trucks, ne evaporative coolers a …õbo…î y…õ ketewa a w…îhy…õ electricity mu.

instruction_en: How do I store my seeds properly for the next planting season?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na meguina m'aba yie ma bere a …õto so?
response_en: Dry seeds thoroughly to less than 10% moisture. Sort and remove damaged, diseased, or small seeds. Place in airtight containers ‚Äî glass jars, tins, or sealed plastic bags. Add a small packet of silica gel or a tablespoon of powdered milk wrapped in cloth to absorb moisture. Store in a cool, dark, dry place. Label with crop name, variety, and date. Test germination before planting season.
response_tw: Yaw aba no yie na …õsua nsukuro 10%. Si na yi aba a as…õe, a yade…õ aka, anaa a w…îkum. De aba to nkuku a apo so ‚Äî glass jar, tin, anaa sealed plastik bag. De silica gel kotoku ketewa anaa powdered milk tablespoon baako a w…îde ntama s…õ…õ no ka ho s…õ …õsuo nsukuro. Guina w…î bae a …õhuru, …õsum, na yera. Kyer…õw nnuaba din, aba din, ne da. Hw…õ aba pue ansa na bere aba.

instruction_en: Where can I sell my farm produce for a better price?
instruction_tw: ∆êhe na metumi de m'afuo nnuaba t…în na nya bo…î pa?
response_en: Sell directly at local markets, to aggregators or food processing companies, or form a cooperative with other farmers. Register with government programs like PFJ which helps connect farmers to buyers.
response_tw: T…în t…ît…î w…î aguabasa a …õb…õn wo, anaa w…î aduan y…õ ade…õ kuo, anaa si cooperative ne okuafo fofor…î. Kyer…õw wo din w…î …îman adwuma te s…õ PFJ a …õboa okuafo ay…înkofa ne ade…õt…îfo.

instruction_en: How do I access credit for farming?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menya ka s…õ mey…õ okuafo adwuma?
response_en: Access agricultural loans through GIRSAL, rural banks, microfinance institutions, and cooperatives. Have a farm business plan ready, register your farm officially, and consult your local MOFA office for guidance.
response_tw: Nya okuafo adwuma ka w…î GIRSAL, …îmansin ban, microfinance kuro, ne cooperatives mu. W…î wo afuo adwuma dwumadie plan, kyer…õw wo afuo din w…î …îman h…î yie, na bisa wo MOFA ofisi w…î beae a …õb…õn wo.

instruction_en: How do I calculate the profit from my farm?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na melem m'afuo ho mfaaso?
response_en: Profit equals Total Sales Revenue minus Total Costs. Track all expenses including seeds, fertilizer, labor, transport, and chemicals and all income from sales. Keep a simple notebook or use your phone to record daily expenses.
response_tw: Mfaaso y…õ sika nyinaa a wogye fi t…în no minus sika nyinaa a wode. Kora sika nyinaa a wode ka ho te s…õ aba, ferefere, dwumadie, kwan so sika, dawuro, ne sika nyinaa a wogye fi t…în. Kora krataa ketewa anaa fa wo f…în de kora da biara ho sika.

instruction_en: Should I join a farmer group or cooperative?
instruction_tw: ∆ês…õ s…õ meba okuafo kuo mu anaa?
response_en: Yes, cooperatives offer many benefits including group purchasing of inputs at lower prices, collective selling for better prices, access to credit, shared equipment, training, and stronger negotiating power with buyers and government.
response_tw: Aane, cooperative de mfaaso bebree br…õ: kuo to adenne…õ ketewa mu, kuo t…în s…õ wanya bo…î pa, nya ka, fa egyapade bom, nyansap…î, na kuo tumi y…õ k…õse sen w…î ade…õt…îfo ne …îman ho ns…õm mu.

instruction_en: How do I add value to my farm produce to earn more money?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mede bo…î ka m'afuo nnuaba ho s…õ menya sika dodo?
response_en: Process raw produce into higher-value products: dry and package grains, make tomato paste, gari from cassava, or palm oil from palm fruits. Value addition reduces post-harvest losses and increases income.
response_tw: Y…õ aduan foforo no y…õ aduan a w…ît…î bo…î k…õse: yaw na paka nnuaba a ayaw, y…õ ntomato paste, gari fi bankye, anaa ngo fi abe aba. Bo…î to so maa mpampa post-harvest mpoano ne ma sika dodo.

instruction_en: How do I take my produce to a market that is far away?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mede m'nnuaba k…î aguabasa a …õnk…î ase?
response_en: Use ventilated crates or baskets not plastic bags for vegetables and fruits. Harvest early morning and transport before midday heat. Avoid overloading to prevent bruising. Cover produce with wet sacks to keep cool. For grains use clean dry bags. Sell same day for perishables whenever possible.
response_tw: Fa afidie a mframa pue mu anaa akete na mfa plastik bags ns…õ nnuan ne aba. Gye owia da ano na de k…î ansa na awia mu ahuhuro aba. B…î hw…õ de dodo hye mu s…õ …õnhow. De ntama nte s…õ…õ nnuaba so s…õ …õkora w…în ase huru. Nnuaba a ayaw ho, fa bags horo ne kraen.

instruction_en: What is a market information system and how does it help farmers?
instruction_tw: D…õn na aguabasa ns…õm system y…õ na …õboa okuafo s…õn?
response_en: A market information system provides farmers with current prices of crops at different markets so they can decide where and when to sell for the best price. In Ghana, services like Esoko, the Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX), and MOFA market information services provide this. Access via phone, radio, or internet. This prevents farmers from being cheated by middlemen who know prices but farmers don't.
response_tw: Aguabasa Ns…õm system de nnuaba bo…î a …õw…î aguabasa ahodo…î so ma okuafo s…õ w…îtumi paw beae ne bere a w…îb…õt…în s…õ wanya bo…î pa. W…î Ghana, nnwuma te s…õ Esoko, Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX), ne MOFA aguabasa ns…õm nnwuma de eyi br…õ. Nya ase w…î f…în, redio, anaa internet so. Eyi mma okuafo anko ase w…î middlemen a w…înim bo…î nanso okuafo nnim ntam.

instruction_en: What is crop insurance and can a Ghanaian farmer access it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na nnuaba ho nsir…õ y…õ na okuafo Ghana tumi nya ase anaa?
response_en: Crop insurance protects farmers against losses from drought, floods, pests, or other disasters by paying compensation when crops fail. In Ghana, the Ghana Agricultural Insurance Pool (GAIP) offers index-based weather insurance for farmers. Premiums are affordable and payouts are based on weather data not individual farm assessment. Contact MOFA or your cooperative to learn how to enroll.
response_tw: Nnuaba ho nsir…õ kora okuafo fi nnuaba ko a …õba fi nsuo pa, nsuo tenten, adwummakers, anaa …îk…îm fofor…î ho w…î de sika br…õ w…în bere a nnuaba nwu. W…î Ghana, Ghana Agricultural Insurance Pool (GAIP) de index-based weather insurance ma okuafo. Premium no bo…î y…õ ketewa na w…îde …õwim ns…õm mmu ka ne afuo biara nhw…õ. Ka kyer…õ MOFA anaa wo cooperative s…õ wuhu …õde…õn na wosi wo din.

instruction_en: How do I negotiate a better price for my farm produce?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mesiesie m'afuo nnuaba bo…î yie?
response_en: To price fairly, calculate your total production cost per unit (cost of seeds, fertilizer, labor, transport, etc. divided by total harvest). Add a profit margin of at least 20-30%. Check current market prices for similar produce in your area. Your price should cover all costs plus give you profit. Never sell below your production cost even if market prices are low.
response_tw: S…õ wohwehw…õ siesie bo…î yie a, lem wo adwuma ho sika nyinaa w…î nnuaba biara so (aba, ferefere, dwumadie, kwan so sika, ne de…õ aka no, ky…õ nyinaa w…î nnuaba a wogye no nyinaa so). Ka mfaaso a …õsua 20-30% ho. Hw…õ bo…î a w…îde t…în nnuaba te s…õ wo de w…î wo beae. Wo bo…î s…õ akata sika nyinaa a wode na ma wo mfaaso. Mto da w…î wo adwuma ho sika ase bere a aguabasa bo…î ay…õ sua.

instruction_en: What government support programmes are available for farmers in Ghana?
instruction_tw: ∆Üman boa adwuma b…õn na …õw…î ho ma okuafo w…î Ghana?
response_en: Key government programs for farmers in Ghana include: Planting for Food and Jobs (PFJ) which provides subsidized seeds and fertilizers; Rearing for Food and Jobs for livestock; Ghana Commercial Agriculture Project (GCAP) for commercial farmers; Youth in Agriculture Program; and the Agricultural Development Bank (ADB) loans. Contact your district MOFA office for current programs available in your area.
response_tw: ∆Üman adwuma titiriw a …õw…î ho ma okuafo w…î Ghana ne: Planting for Food and Jobs (PFJ) a …õde aba ne ferefere a w…îatwa bo…î br…õ; Rearing for Food and Jobs ma mmoa; Ghana Commercial Agriculture Project (GCAP) ma t…înt…în okuafo; Youth in Agriculture Program; ne Agricultural Development Bank (ADB) ka. Ka kyer…õ wo district MOFA ofisi ma adwuma a …õw…î h…î w…î wo beae saa ara.

instruction_en: What is the Ghana Commodity Exchange and how do I use it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na Ghana Commodity Exchange y…õ na …õde…õn na mefa di dwuma?
response_en: The Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX) is a formal marketplace where farmers can sell their produce at transparent, standardized prices. To use it: register as a member through an accredited warehouse operator, store your certified grain in a GCX-accredited warehouse, receive a warehouse receipt that you can sell at the exchange or use as collateral for a bank loan. It is especially good for maize, soya, and sorghum.
response_tw: Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX) y…õ aguabasa a …õw…î ho yie a okuafo tumi t…în w…în nnuaba w…î bo…î a w…îada adi ne a w…îasiesie. S…õ wohwehw…õ fa di dwuma a, kyer…õw wo din w…î accredited warehouse operator ho, guina wo nnuaba a w…îakyer…õw w…î GCX-accredited warehouse mu, nya warehouse receipt a wobetumi t…în w…î exchange no mu anaa de k…î ban ho s…õ collateral. ∆êy…õ adwuma yie pa ara ma aburow, soya, ne …ît…õ.

instruction_en: How do I protect my farm during heavy rainfall?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mehw…õ m'afuo so bere a nsuo pa?
response_en: During drought, mulch heavily to conserve soil moisture, irrigate if possible, choose drought-tolerant crop varieties, and avoid adding nitrogen fertilizer which increases water demand. Harvest early-maturing varieties before drought peaks.
response_tw: Bere a nsuo pa no, de mulch to asase so yiye s…õ nsukuro wo mu, de nsuo to nnua so s…õ wonya tumi, paw nnuaba aba a …õtumi nsuo pa so, na b…î hw…õ de nitrogen ferefere to s…õ …õma nnuaba hia nsuo dodo. Gye nnuaba a w…îware nt…õm ansa na nsuo pa y…õ dodo.

instruction_en: Heavy rain has flooded my farm. What do I do?
instruction_tw: Osu tenten ma nsuo hy…õ…õ m'afuo mu. D…õn na mey…õ?
response_en: After flooding, make drainage channels to remove excess water quickly. Allow soil to drain before entering with heavy machinery. Check plants for root rot and remove severely damaged ones. Apply potassium fertilizer to help plants recover. Be alert for increased fungal diseases after flooding.
response_tw: Nsuo tu akyi, y…õ mm…înkyemmonkyem s…õ nsuo no pue nt…õm. Ma asase no ntena ansa na wode egyapade k…õse ba mu. Hw…õ nnuaba ntini ho s…õ w…îas…õe na yi a w…îas…õe k…õse. De potassium ferefere to so s…õ …õboa nnuaba no sane aba.

instruction_en: Strong wind has lodged my maize plants. Can they recover?
instruction_tw: Mframa tumi buu m'aburow nnua. W…îtumi sane aba anaa?
response_en: Lodging from wind can be managed by carefully straightening and mounding soil around fallen plants within 24-48 hours. However if the stem is broken recovery is unlikely. To prevent lodging choose short-stalked varieties and avoid excessive nitrogen.
response_tw: Wobetumi hw…õ nnua a mframa ato w…în ase no ase w…î de asase de si w…în ho p…õ w…î dafe 24-48 mu. Nanso s…õ tibo no apae a, …õny…õ …îkor…î s…õ w…îsane ba. S…õ wohwehw…õ kora ase a, paw aba a w…în tiboo tia na kora nitrogen ferefere ase.

instruction_en: How do I know when rain is coming so I can plan my farming?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menim bere a osu reba?
response_en: Watch for natural signs such as flowering of certain trees, behavior of birds, and increased humidity. Check Ghana Meteorological Agency forecasts on radio, TV, or their website. Local MOFA extension officers often share seasonal forecasts. Planting too early before rains establish risks seed loss.
response_tw: Hw…õ nhwiren foforo ns…õm te s…õ nnua bi abode, anomaa biribi, ne huhu a …õresor. Hw…õ Ghana Meteorological Agency amanne…õ w…î redio, TV, anaa w…în website so. MOFA extension officers a …õb…õn wo no taa ky…õ bere amanne…õ. To aba nt…õm dodo ansa na osu reba retwe aba no s…õe.

instruction_en: How do I manage crops that are not performing well when I have no money?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mehw…õ nhaban a …õntia as…õm yie a mennya sika koraa?
response_en: Control weeds by timely hand weeding in the first 3-4 weeks after planting, using mulch to suppress weeds, and applying pre-emergence herbicides at planting. The first weeding must be done within 2-3 weeks after crop emergence.
response_tw: Hw…õ nhaban foforo ase w…î nnafoa 3-4 a aburow pue akyi no mu, fa mulch kora nhaban foforo ase, na de herbicide to ansa na nnuaba pue. Nhaban foforo tia a …õdi …õkan no y…õ w…î nnafoa 2-3 mu a nnuaba pue akyi.

instruction_en: What is intercropping and how does it benefit my farm?
instruction_tw: D…õn na …õy…õ intercropping na …õboa?
response_en: Intercropping means growing two or more crops together on the same land. For example, maize with cowpea. Benefits include better land use, the legume adds nitrogen to the soil, reduced pest pressure, and income from two crops.
response_tw: Intercropping kyer…õ s…õ wokura nnuaba abien anaa dodo w…î asase koro so. Nhw…õso so, aburow ne ab…îdwe…õ. Mfaaso ho ne: asase ho dwuma pa, ab…îdwe…õ de nitrogen ma asase, adwummakers hia sua, na nnuaba abien firi sika.

instruction_en: How often should I weed my farm?
instruction_tw: Mmmere ahe na ms…õ mey…õ nhaban foforo tia w…î m'afuo mu?
response_en: Weed at least twice per season ‚Äî first weeding within 2-3 weeks of planting and second weeding 4-6 weeks after planting. After this, crop canopy usually shades out weeds. Maize is most sensitive to weeds in the first 30-40 days after planting.
response_tw: Tia nhaban foforo mmere abien p…õ w…î bere biara ‚Äî nhaban tia a …õdi …õkan w…î nnawotwe 2-3 a wode aba to mu akyi na nhaban tia a …õto so abien w…î nnawotwe 4-6 akyi. Eyi akyi, nnuaba nhaban taa s…õ…õ nhaban foforo ase.

instruction_en: How do I keep records for my farm?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mekora m'afuo ns…õm?
response_en: Keep a simple farm diary or notebook. Record planting dates, inputs purchased and costs, labor hired and paid, crop activities, harvest quantities, and sales. Simple mobile apps or voice memos on your phone can help record activities easily.
response_tw: Kora afuo krataa anaa ns…õmpoma ketewa. Kora aba to da, adenne…õ a wot…î ne won bo…î, dwumadie a wohw…õ ne sika a wode twaa, afuo adwuma, nnuaba a wogye, ne t…în ns…õm. Mobile apps ketewa anaa kasa nkrataa w…î wo f…în no tumi boa wo kora ns…õm yie.

instruction_en: How do I calculate the size of my farm in acres?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na menim m'afuo bo…î w…î acres mu?
response_en: Measure your farm using a measuring tape, GPS app like Google Maps, or by counting paces where 100 paces by 100 paces is approximately 0.2 acres. Alternatively your local MOFA office can help measure your farm.
response_tw: Sus wo afuo w…î susudua, GPS app te s…õ Google Maps anaa OsmAnd, anaa kan t…înt…în a t…înt…în 100 ne t…înt…în 100 y…õ b…õn acres 0.2. Anoa, wo MOFA ofisi a …õb…õn wo no tumi boa wo su wo afuo.

instruction_en: How do I manage my farm operations efficiently?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mehw…õ afuo dwumadie yie?
response_en: Manage farm labor by: planning activities in advance and knowing how many workers you need for each task, hiring skilled workers for technical tasks and using family labor for routine work, paying fair wages on time to retain good workers, organizing communal labor (nnoboa) with neighboring farmers to reduce costs, tracking time spent on each activity to identify inefficiencies, and using appropriate tools to reduce manual labor.
response_tw: Hw…õ afuo dwumadie w…î: dwumadie siesie ansa na …õba na nim obi dodo a wohia ma adwuma biara, fa obi a onim adwuma ma adwuma a …õhia nyansam na fa abusua adwuma ma adwuma a w…îtaa y…õ, tua dwumadie sika a …õfata bere a …õs…õ s…õ wokora adwumfo pa, si nnoboa ne okuafo a w…îb…õn wo s…õ mpampa sika, kora bere a w…îde y…õ adwuma biara so s…õ wuhu beae a enni adwuma pa, na fa ade…õ a …õfata di dwuma s…õ mpampa nsa adwuma.

instruction_en: What is a farm map and why should I make one?
instruction_tw: D…õn na afuo map y…õ na …õs…õ s…õ mey…õ baako ad…õn?
response_en: A farm map is a simple drawing or diagram showing your farm layout including field boundaries, crops in each area, water sources, access roads, trees, and infrastructure. It helps you: plan crop rotations, track what was planted where, plan irrigation and drainage, apply for land certificates, and communicate with extension officers and banks. You can draw it by hand or use free GPS apps on your phone.
response_tw: Afuo map y…õ kyer…õwee anaa diagram ketewa a …õkyer…õ wo afuo anamm…în a mu w…î afuo nky…õn, nnuaba a …õw…î beae biara mu, nsuo, kwan, nnua, ne adwumafie. ∆êboa wo s…õ: siesie nnuaba sesae, kora ns…õm a wode aba to mu w…î beae b…õn, siesie nsuo to ne nsuo pue anamm…în, bisa asase krataa, na kasa ne extension officers ne ban. Wobetumi kyer…õw wo nsa so anaa fa GPS apps daa w…î wo f…în so.

instruction_en: How do I reduce post-harvest losses on my farm?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na metumi sua mpoano a …õba fi kwan so anamm…în b…îne ho?
response_en: Reduce transport losses by: harvesting at the right maturity, grading and sorting produce before transport, using appropriate packaging (ventilated crates for fresh produce, strong sacks for grains), loading carefully to prevent crushing, traveling during cool hours (early morning or night), using roads that avoid excessive vibration, and selling as close to the farm as possible to reduce transport distance.
response_tw: Sua kwan so mpoano w…î: gye nnuaba bere a …õs…õ, si ne yi nnuaba ansa na wede k…î, fa packaging a …õfata (afidie a mframa pue mu ma nnuaba foforo, kotoku den ma nnuaba a ayaw), de adwuma yie s…õ …õnhow, k…î bere a …õhuru (owia da ano anaa anadwo), fa kwan a wonyi nnuaba no dodo, na t…în a …õb…õn afuo so ara s…õ sua kwan asukyer…õ.

instruction_en: What is precision farming and can a small farmer use it?
instruction_tw: D…õn na precision farming y…õ na okuafo ketewa tumi y…õ?
response_en: Precision farming means using data and technology to apply exactly the right amount of inputs at the right time and place. Smallholders can practice basic precision farming by: soil testing different parts of the field and applying fertilizer based on results, using mobile apps to track crop growth, monitoring pest and disease hotspots and targeting treatment, and keeping detailed farm records to identify what works best.
response_tw: Precision farming kyer…õ fa data ne technology di dwuma s…õ de adenne…õ a …õfata to bere a …õs…õ na beae a …õs…õ. Okuafo ketewa tumi y…õ basic precision farming w…î: hw…õ asase ahorow afuo mu na de ferefere to w…î adwuma ho, fa mobile apps di dwuma s…õ kora nnuaba da sor, hw…õ adwumakers ne yade…õ beae na hy…õ dawuro h…î p…õ, na kora afuo ns…õm mu yiye s…õ wuhu dea y…õ adwuma pa.

instruction_en: How do I plan and prepare for the farming season?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mesiesie bere a ansa na …õba?
response_en: Season planning should include: checking your budget and available resources, deciding which crops to grow based on market demand and land suitability, calculating input quantities needed and ordering in advance, arranging labor supply for peak periods, planning irrigation if needed, identifying buyers in advance, and reviewing what worked and what didn't in the previous season. Start planning at least 2 months before the season begins.
response_tw: Bere siesie s…õ aka: hw…õ wo sika ne adenne…õ a wow…î, paw nnuaba a wob…õkura w…î aguabasa hia ne asase ho, lem adenne…õ dodo a wohia na t…î ansa na bere aba, siesie obi a ob…õy…õ adwuma w…î bere a …õhia dodo, siesie nsuo to s…õ …õhia, hwehw…õ ade…õt…îfo ansa na bere aba, na hw…õ dea y…õ adwuma yie ne dea any…õ yie w…î bere a edi …õkan. Fi siesie abosome 2 ansa na bere aba.

instruction_en: How do I manage waterlogging on my farm?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mehw…õ nsuo a …õhy…õ m'afuo mu?
response_en: If your farm floods regularly, take these steps: dig drainage channels around and through the field to direct water away, create raised beds to keep plant roots above waterline, plant flood-tolerant crop varieties, install water pumps to remove standing water quickly, build earthen bunds around the field perimeter, and consider planting floating rice varieties if flooding is very severe and frequent.
response_tw: S…õ nsuo taa hye wo afuo mu a, y…õ eyi: hu mm…înkyemmonkyem twa ne w…î afuo mu de nsuo no k…î, y…õ asase-sor afuo s…õ nnuaba ntini atena sor nsuo soronko, to aba a …õtumi nsuo tenten ase hy…õ, si nsuo pumps s…õ yi nsuo a …õda h…î nt…õm, y…õ asase b…î twa afuo no ho, na hw…õ fa floating rice aba s…õ nsuo tenten taa ba na …õy…õ dodo.

instruction_en: How do I protect my farm from thieves?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mekora m'afuo fi kor…îmfo ho?
response_en: Protect your farm by: building proper fencing with thorny hedges or wire fencing, using watchdogs especially at harvest time, forming community vigilance groups with neighboring farmers, harvesting promptly when crops are ready to minimize field time after ripening, installing basic alarm systems if affordable, registering your farm to establish legal ownership, and developing good relationships with neighboring community members.
response_tw: Kora wo afuo w…î: si fense yie w…î nkyer…õ nhwiren anaa wire, fa …îkraman kora titiriw bere a wogye nnuaba, si kuo ne okuafo a w…îb…õn wo s…õ w…îhw…õ so, gye nnuaba nt…õm bere a …õawie ase s…õ afuo mu bere sua akyi a nnuaba awi, si basic alarm s…õ sika w…î ho, kyer…õw wo afuo din s…õ wunya asase krataa yie, na y…õ abak…îs…õm yie ne oman mu nnipa a w…îb…õn wo.

instruction_en: How do I make neem pesticide spray at home?
instruction_tw: ∆êde…õn na mey…õ neem adwummakers dawuro w…î fie?
response_en: Crush 500g of neem seeds, soak in 10 litres of water overnight, strain through cloth, and add a little soap. Spray on crops in early morning or evening. Neem controls aphids, whiteflies, and caterpillars. Reapply after rain.
response_tw: Tutu neem aba 500g, da w…î nsuo litre 10 mu anadwo koro, hun w…î ntama mu, na de sapo kakra ka ho. Guare nnuaba so owia da ano anaa anwummere. Neem kora aphid, fitaa abowa, ne abor…înoma ase yie. De to so bio nsuo akyi.

instruction_en: What is organic farming?
instruction_tw: D…õn na nhwiren foforo okuafo adwuma y…õ?
