The grain moisture at harvest should be around 25 to 30 percent. For dry storage, allow the cobs to dry further until moisture drops to 13 percent.
response_tw: Aburo…î atwa so s…õ w…îb…õ yi adays 90 k…î 120 akyi a wode aba to mu, s…õ variety no so. Nsono a …õkyer…õ s…õ …õatwa so ne: silks brown ne w…î afahye a atew, nsono tuntum k…õse w…î aba no ase bere a wuyi mu, na husks no atew s…õ ntama. Nsuo a …õw…î aba mu bere a wuyi ne b…õy…õ 25 k…î 30 percent. Ma no tew bio k…îsi s…õ nsuo besi 13 percent ansa na woguina mu.

instruction_en: How do I store harvested maize to prevent losses?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ aburo…î a mayie no so s…õ …õremma ahunu?
response_en: Dry the maize thoroughly until moisture content drops below 13 percent. Shell the dried cobs and store the grain in sealed metal silos, hermetic bags or plastic drums. Add approved grain protectants like Actellic Super to prevent weevil damage. Keep the storage area dry, well ventilated and raised off the ground. Inspect monthly and remove any mouldy grains immediately.
response_tw: Ma aburo…î no tew yie kosi s…õ nsuo a …õw…î mu sian 13 percent ase. Shell cobs a …õatew no na guina aba no w…î metal silos a w…îato mu, hermetic bags anaa plastic drums. De grain protectants a w…îagyae so te s…õ Actellic Super ka ho s…õ weevils nnyi. Ma store no y…õ d…õ, boa mframa na k…î so asase so. Hw…õ so s…õ w…îb…õk…î b…î wo dua mu na yi aba a mold aba ho nt…õm.

instruction_en: What are the common diseases of maize in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Yade…õ b…õn na …õtaa ba aburo…î ho w…î Ghana?
response_en: Common maize diseases in Ghana include maize streak virus which causes yellow streaks on leaves, grey leaf spot which creates rectangular grey lesions, northern corn leaf blight causing large tan lesions, and maize lethal necrosis. Use resistant varieties from CSIR, practice crop rotation, remove infected plants early, and avoid planting in waterlogged areas to reduce disease incidence.
response_tw: Yade…õ a …õtaa ba aburo…î ho w…î Ghana ne: maize streak virus a …õma nhaban no ho y…õ yellow, grey leaf spot a …õma nhaban ho y…õ grey, northern corn leaf blight a …õma nsono k…õse tan aba, na maize lethal necrosis. De varieties a w…îagyae so fi CSIR, sesa adekuo adwuma, yi nnuaba a yade…õ aba ho nt…õm, na mma aba to mu w…î nsuo mu agyina ho.

instruction_en: How many bags of maize can I expect from one acre?
instruction_tw: Sacks aburo…î ahe na mebetumi anya fi eka baako mu?
response_en: With good management, improved varieties and proper fertilization, you can harvest 20 to 25 bags of 100 kg each per acre. Without fertilizer and using local varieties, expect 8 to 12 bags per acre. The key factors affecting yield are soil fertility, seed variety, timely planting, weed control, pest management and rainfall adequacy.
response_tw: S…õ woy…õ adwuma pa, de varieties pa to mu na de ferefere pa to ho a, wobetumi anya sacks 20 k…î 25 a nn…îho 100 kg biara w…î eka baako mu. S…õ wonni ferefere na wode local varieties to mu a, gyina sacks 8 k…î 12 biara eka baako ho. As…õde a …õma aburo…î reb…õ y…õ ne: asase no aduan, aba variety, bere pa mu to aba, nhaban foforo yi, adwummaker hw…õ, na nsuo pa.

instruction_en: Can I intercrop maize with other crops?
instruction_tw: Metumi de aburo…î ato mu ne nnuaba fofor…î?
response_en: Yes, maize intercropping is common and beneficial in Ghana. You can intercrop maize with cowpea, groundnut, cassava or beans. Cowpea and groundnut fix nitrogen into the soil which benefits the maize. Cassava can be planted at the same time but needs wider spacing. Avoid intercropping with crops that are too tall or spread wide as they will shade the maize.
response_tw: Aane, aburo…î ne nnuaba fofor…î to mu y…õ biribi a w…îy…õ na …õboa w…î Ghana. Wobetumi de aburo…î ato mu ne b…îde, groundnut, bankye anaa aduaba. B…îde ne groundnut de nitrogen ka asase mu a …õboa aburo…î. Wobetumi de bankye to mu bere koro nanso …õhia ahod…î k…õse. Mma aba to mu ne nnuaba a …õsua f…õw anaa a ahorow tete yie efis…õ w…îb…õkata aburo…î no so.

instruction_en: How do I treat maize seed before planting?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ aburo…î aba ansa na meto mu?
response_en: Treat maize seed with a fungicide and insecticide seed dressing before planting to protect against soil-borne diseases and early insect attack. Products like Apron Star or Gaucho are commonly used. Coat the seeds lightly, allow them to dry for 30 minutes and plant immediately. Buy certified seeds from reputable agro-input dealers to ensure good germination rates.
response_tw: De fungicide ne insecticide seed dressing to aburo…î aba so ansa na wode to mu s…õ …õhw…õ aba no so fi asase yade…õ ne adwummaker atia a …õdi kan. Nne…õma te s…õ Apron Star anaa Gaucho na w…îde taa y…õ. Kata aba no so kakra, ma no tew simma 30 na to mu nt…õm. Gye certified seeds fi agro-input dealers a w…îni gyinae so s…õ aba atua f…îw yie.

instruction_en: How do I select good cassava stems for planting?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mepick bankye ab…î pa s…õ mede to mu?
response_en: Select stems from healthy mature cassava plants that are 8 to 12 months old. The stem should be firm, not shrivelled, about 2 to 3 cm thick and 20 to 30 cm long with at least 5 nodes. Avoid stems from diseased plants or those showing mosaic virus symptoms. Store cuttings in the shade for no more than 3 days before planting.
response_tw: Yi ab…î fi bankye nnuaba pa a w…îawo akyi mfee 8 k…î 12. Ab…î no nni yie, mmaadwa gu ho, b…õy…õ s…õntimita 2 k…î 3 tiawa na s…õntimita 20 k…î 30 tenten a …õw…î nodes 5 p…õ. Gye wo ho fi ab…î a …õfi nnuaba a yade…õ aba ho anaa a …õkyer…õ mosaic virus ho nsono. Guina cutting no w…î hare ha mpr…õnsa mmi…õnsa p…õ ansa na wode to mu.

instruction_en: What is the best way to plant cassava stems?
instruction_tw: D…õn na …õy…õ …õkwan pa s…õ mede bankye ab…î to mu?
response_en: Plant cassava stems at an angle of 45 degrees or vertically in the soil. Bury at least two to three nodes underground leaving three or more above ground. The spacing should be 1 metre by 1 metre for food production, or 0.8 by 1 metre for commercial production. Plant at the start of the rainy season when soil moisture is adequate. Planting upright gives higher yields but angled planting is easier to harvest.
response_tw: De bankye ab…î to mu w…î angle 45 degrees anaa straight w…î asase mu. Fa mu s…õ nodes mmienu k…î mmi…õnsa b…õk…î asase mu na nnua mmi…õnsa anaa saa k…î so b…õsi asase so. Ahod…î nni obi metre baako ne metre baako ho ma aduan na 0.8 ne metre baako ma adwuma k…õse. To mu nsuo ber…õ akyirikyiri bere a asase mu nsuo ho hia. Straight to aba mu kyer…õ nnoa pii nanso angled nna hia adwuma pii bere a wuyi.

instruction_en: How long does cassava take to mature?
instruction_tw: Bankye twa so s…õ w…îb…õyi no ahe?
response_en: Cassava matures between 8 to 24 months depending on the variety. Early maturing varieties like Afisiafi take 8 to 12 months while late maturing varieties can take 18 to 24 months. CSIR improved varieties like Afisiafi, Bankye Hemaa and Abasafitaa mature in 9 to 12 months and give high yields. Cassava left in the ground beyond maturity becomes woody and loses starch content.
response_tw: Bankye twa so w…î s…õ mfee 8 k…î 24 mu s…õ variety no so. Varieties a …õtwa so nt…õm te s…õ Afisiafi twa so s…õ mfee 8 k…î 12 na varieties a …õtwa so nk…î…î b…õtumi ak…îsi mfee 18 k…î 24. response_en: Major cassava pests include cassava mealybug which causes wilting and stunted growth, cassava green mite causing leaf bronzing, and cassava whitefly which spreads mosaic virus. Control mealybug by releasing natural enemies like the parasitoid wasp Anagyrus lopezi. Remove affected plants and use clean planting material. response_en: Cassava mosaic disease causes yellow mosaic pattern on leaves and reduces yield severely. Cassava brown streak causes brown streaks on stems and rotting of the tuber. Cassava root rot causes tuber decay. Use virus-free certified planting material, plant resistant varieties, remove and destroy infected plants early, and avoid replanting in infected fields for at least one season.

instruction_en: How much profit can I make from one acre of cassava?

response_en: From one acre of cassava using improved varieties, you can harvest 8 to 15 tonnes of fresh roots. At a farm gate price of GHS 150 to 200 per bag, you can earn GHS 3,000 to 6,000 per acre after deducting input and labour costs of about GHS 800 to 1,200. Cassava farming is most profitable when you process it into gari, starch or fufu flour before selling.

instruction_en: How do I start a plantain farm in Ghana?

Apply compost or well-rotted manure to each hole before planting. Plant the sucker upright, cover with topsoil and firm gently. Water if there is no rain. instruction_en: What is the difference between plantain and banana farming?

Banana requires more water and is more sensitive to drought. Plantain is more tolerant of less fertile soils. Both need similar disease management. Commercial banana farming for export requires higher standards of pest and disease control.

instruction_en: How do I prepare yam setts for planting?

In areas with two rainy seasons, a second planting can be done in September. Yam planted too late misses the best growing period and gives poor yields.

response_en: Late blight caused by Phytophthora infestans is the most destructive tomato disease. Prevent it by using resistant varieties, avoiding overhead irrigation, ensuring good spacing for air circulation, removing and destroying infected plant material immediately, and applying copper-based fungicides like Ridomil or Champion preventively especially during wet humid weather.

Fix it by ensuring consistent irrigation so the plant can absorb calcium evenly, applying calcium nitrate or lime to the soil, avoiding excess nitrogen fertilizer which interferes with calcium uptake, and mulching to retain soil moisture.

instruction_en: How do I grow onions in Ghana?

Hw…õ s…õ ahod…î pa s…õntimita 10 k…î 15 w…î h…î, de ferefere a w…îagyae so to ho, yi improved bulb onion varieties te s…õ Bawku Red anaa Onex, na hw…õ quench yie.

instruction_en: How do I grow carrots in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ meto carrot w…î Ghana?
response_en: Carrots grow best in loose, deep, stone-free sandy loam soils. Prepare the bed by digging deep to at least 30 cm and removing stones and clods. Sow seeds directly in rows 20 cm apart at a depth of 1 cm. Thin seedlings to 5 to 8 cm apart after germination. Apply phosphorus-rich fertilizer at planting. Carrots need consistent moisture but not waterlogging. They are ready to harvest in 70 to 80 days.
response_tw: Carrot to yie w…î asase a …õte, tia, na abo nni mu sandy loam asase. Siesie bed no de boro tia s…õ s…õntimita 30 na yi abo ne nsono. Wui aba straight w…î rows a s…õntimita 20 ntam w…î tia s…õntimita baako. Yi seedlings k…îsi s…õ s…õntimita 5 k…î 8 ntam akyi a w…îatua f…îw. De ferefere a phosphorus w…î mu to ho bere a wode to mu. Carrot hia nsuo so yie nanso nsuo nni mu agyina. ∆êtwa so s…õ w…îb…õyi adays 70 k…î 80 mu.

instruction_en: How do I grow garden eggs in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ meto ntor…î w…î Ghana?
response_en: Garden eggs also called African eggplant are popular in Ghana. Start seedlings in a nursery for 4 weeks. Transplant to the field at 60 by 60 cm spacing. Apply compost at planting and NPK fertilizer at 3 weeks after transplanting. Control the epilachna beetle which is the most destructive pest by hand picking or using neem extract. Garden eggs mature in 60 to 90 days and can be harvested multiple times from the same plant.
response_tw: Ntor…î a w…îfre no African eggplant nso ne aduan a w…îp…õ w…î Ghana. Fi ase de aba to mu w…î nursery nnansa nnan. Transplant k…î afuom w…î ahod…î 60 ne 60 s…õntimita. De compost to ho bere a wode to mu na NPK ferefere nnansa mmi…õnsa akyi a woransplant. Boa epilachna beetle a …õs…õe paa ho de nsa yi anaa de neem extract hware. Ntor…î twa so s…õ w…îb…õyi adays 60 k…î 90 mu na wubetumi ayi fi nnuaba koro mu daa.

instruction_en: How do I establish a palm oil plantation in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mefi ase ab…õ afuom w…î Ghana?
response_en: Select a site with deep well-drained soils and rainfall above 1500 mm annually. Obtain certified tenera seedlings from CSIR-Oil Palm Research Institute at Kusi. Plant at 9 by 9 metres triangular spacing giving about 143 trees per hectare. Apply NPK fertilizer twice yearly. Palms begin producing after 3 to 4 years and remain productive for 25 to 30 years. Join the Ghana Oil Palm Development Company cooperative for support and guaranteed market.
response_tw: Yi asase a …õw…î tia yie ne nsuo tew na nsuo a …õt…î biara afe hy…õ 1500 mm so. Gye certified tenera seedlings fi CSIR-Oil Palm Research Institute w…î Kusi. De to mu w…î 9 ne 9 metres triangular ahod…î a …õma nnuaba 143 w…î hectare biara mu. De NPK ferefere to ho mpr…õn mmienu biara afe. Ab…õ fi ase w…î aduane mfe mmi…õnsa k…î nnan akyi na …õgyina so w…î mfe 25 k…î 30. Ka Ghana Oil Palm Development Company kuo ho ma w…îboa wo ne dwa gyinae.

instruction_en: How do I grow coconut trees in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ meto kuuku nnuaba w…î Ghana?
response_en: Coconut grows best along coastal areas and in areas with high rainfall. Plant whole mature coconuts or purchase nursery seedlings. Dig a hole 1 metre deep and wide, add compost to the base and plant the seedling. Space at 7 to 10 metres apart. Coconut begins bearing fruit after 6 to 10 years depending on variety. Dwarf varieties like Malayan Dwarf produce earlier at 3 to 4 years. Apply potassium-rich fertilizer for good fruit production.
response_tw: Kuuku to yie w…î mpoano ho ne h…î a nsuo t…î yie. De kuuku a …õatwa so p…õp…õ…õp…õ to mu anaa gye nursery seedlings. Boro awia a …õtel…õ ne teaa metre baako, de compost to ase na to seedling. Fa ahod…î 7 k…î 10 metres. Kuuku fi ase de nsuo w…î mfe 6 k…î 10 akyi s…õ variety no so. Dwarf varieties te s…õ Malayan Dwarf de nsuo nt…õm w…î mfe mmi…õnsa k…î nnan. De ferefere a potassium w…î mu to ho ma nsuo pa.

instruction_en: How do I start a fish farm in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mefi ase apataa adwuma w…î Ghana?
response_en: Start by choosing your fish species ‚Äî tilapia and catfish are most common in Ghana. Select a site with a reliable water source. You can use earthen ponds, concrete tanks or plastic tanks. Construct ponds of at least 500 square metres for commercial production. Stock fingerlings at the right density ‚Äî for tilapia 3 to 5 fish per square metre and catfish 10 to 20 per square metre. Feed twice daily and harvest in 5 to 6 months.
response_tw: Fi ase de yi wo apataa nhyiamu ‚Äî tilapia ne catfish na w…îtaa to mu w…î Ghana. Yi asase a nsuo w…î so daadaa. Wobetumi de earthen ponds, concrete tanks anaa plastic tanks. Y…õ ponds a …õtel…õ s…õ square metres 500 p…õ ma adwuma k…õse. De fingerlings si w…î measure pa mu ‚Äî ma tilapia, apataa 3 k…î 5 biara square metre mu na catfish 10 k…î 20 biara square metre mu. Didi mpr…õn mmienu biara da na yi apataa mfee 5 k…î 6 mu.

instruction_en: What is the best fish feed for tilapia in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Apataa aduan b…õn na …õhia pa ara ma tilapia w…î Ghana?
response_en: Tilapia does well on commercial pellet feeds with protein content of 25 to 30 percent. Popular brands in Ghana include Raanan, Coppens and Skretting. You can also prepare on-farm feed using locally available ingredients like maize bran, soybean meal, fishmeal and rice bran. Feed at 3 to 5 percent of body weight daily. Reduce feed when water temperature is very high or very low as fish appetite decreases.
response_tw: Tilapia to yie w…î commercial pellet feeds a protein 25 k…î 30 percent w…î mu. Brands a w…îp…õ w…î Ghana ne Raanan, Coppens na Skretting. Wobetumi nso de aduan a …õw…î ho w…î …õh…î y…õ te s…õ aburo…î bran, soybean meal, fishmeal ne rice bran. Didi b…î so biara adays 3 k…î 5 percent. Sua aduan bere a nsuo ber…õ y…õ hyehy…õ paa anaa d…õ paa efis…õ apataa nhia aduan da.

instruction_en: How do I prevent disease in my fish pond?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ yade…õ annyi me apataa ho?
response_en: Prevent fish diseases by maintaining good water quality with regular testing and water changes, avoiding overstocking, feeding quality feed without overfeeding, quarantining new fish for 2 weeks before introducing to main ponds, not mixing fish from unknown sources, and removing dead fish immediately. Common fish diseases include ich (white spot), gill diseases and bacterial infections which can be treated with salt baths and approved veterinary medications.
response_tw: Gye apataa yade…õ fi ho de kora nsuo pa na hw…õ so bere biara na sesa nsuo, gye wo ho fi to pii, didi aduan pa na amma …õnboro so, de apataa fofor…î sie nnansa mmienu ansa na wode ka ho w…î ponds k…õse, mma apataa fi h…î a wonnim fi ka ho, na yi apataa a awu nt…õm. Yade…õ a …õtaa ba apataa ho ne ich (white spot), gill yade…õ ne bacterial infections a wubetumi ahu w…în ho de salt baths ne nkuru a w…îagyae so.

instruction_en: How much does it cost to start a fish farm in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Sika ahe na …õy…õ papa s…õ mefi ase apataa adwuma w…î Ghana?
response_en: Starting a small commercial fish farm in Ghana with two earthen ponds of 500 square metres each costs approximately GHS 15,000 to 25,000 including pond construction, fingerlings, feed for one cycle, and basic equipment. A plastic tank fish farm can be started for GHS 8,000 to 15,000. The main ongoing costs are fish feed which accounts for 60 to 70 percent of total production cost. With good management you can make a profit from the first cycle.
response_tw: Apataa adwuma ketewa k…õse fi ase w…î Ghana de earthen ponds mmienu a square metres 500 biara w…î mu ho teetee GHS 15,000 k…î 25,000 a pond y…õ, fingerlings, aduan w…î cycle baako mu, ne nne…õma a …õy…õ papa. Plastic tank apataa adwuma wobetumi de GHS 8,000 k…î 15,000 fi ase. Nne…õma a …õkyere daadaa a …õk…î so pii ne apataa aduan a …õy…õ adwuma agyapade 60 k…î 70 percent. S…õ woy…õ adwuma pa a, wobetumi anya mfaso w…î cycle a …õdi kan mu.

instruction_en: When should I harvest my tilapia fish?
instruction_tw: Bere b…õn na meyiy…õ me tilapia apataa?
response_en: Harvest tilapia when they reach market size of 300 to 500 grams which typically takes 5 to 6 months from fingerling stage. Monitor growth by sampling fish monthly using a net. Harvest in the early morning when water temperature is cool to reduce fish stress. Partial harvesting at 4 months removes larger fish and allows smaller ones to grow. Sell fresh fish immediately or ice them for transport to market.
response_tw: Yi tilapia bere a w…îk…î si dwa k…õse a nn…îho 300 k…î 500 gram a …õtaa ky…õ mfee 5 k…î 6 fi fingerling mu. Hw…õ s…õ apataa reb…õbo yie de sample apataa bere biara de net. Yi an…îpa bere a nsuo ber…õ d…õ s…õ apataa nhunu. Partial yi w…î mfee nnan mu yi apataa k…õse na ma nketewa bo s…î. T…î apataa fofor nt…õm anaa de ice ka ho ma ak…î dwa.

instruction_en: What is the difference between tilapia and catfish farming?
instruction_tw: Nte s…õn b…õn na …õw…î tilapia ne catfish adwuma ntam?
response_en: Tilapia is easier to manage, tolerates lower water quality, feeds on plankton and algae as well as pellets, and is cheaper to produce. Catfish grows faster, commands a higher market price, tolerates very low oxygen levels but needs more protein in feed. Catfish farming gives higher profit per fish but requires more management. Many farmers in Ghana combine both species for diversified income.
response_tw: Tilapia y…õ mmer…õw s…õ wohw…õ so, …õtumi gyina nsuo a …õnni yie, …õdi plankton ne algae ne pellets nso, na bo w…î to a …õsua. Catfish bo s…î nt…õm, dwa bo y…õ so sen, …õtumi gyina oxygen nketewa a w…î nsuo mu nanso …õhia protein pii w…î aduan mu. Catfish adwuma de mfaso k…õse ba ma apataa baako baako nanso …õhia adwuma pii. Akuafo pii w…î Ghana de nhyiamu mmienu kaa bom s…õ w…înya owuo-teetee foforo.

instruction_en: How do I start a poultry farm in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mefi ase akoko adwuma w…î Ghana?
response_en: Decide whether to raise broilers for meat or layers for eggs. Build or rent a poultry house that is well ventilated, predator-proof and has adequate space of 0.1 square metres per broiler or 0.2 square metres per layer. Source day-old chicks from reputable hatcheries. Provide feed, clean water, light and warmth from a brooder for the first 2 weeks. Vaccinate against Newcastle disease and Gumboro disease. Broilers are ready in 6 to 7 weeks and layers start laying at 18 to 20 weeks.
response_tw: Siesie wo tiri ma broilers w…î nam ho anaa layers w…î tamma ho. Tu anaa sowa akoko fie a mframa fa mu yie, adwummaker ntumi nk…î mu, na ahorow teetee w…î h…î s…õ 0.1 square metres biara broiler anaa 0.2 square metres biara layer. Gye day-old chicks fi hatcheries a w…îni gyinae. De aduan, nsuo pa, kanea ne ber…õhy…õ fi brooder ma nnansa mmienu a …õdi kan. Hware w…î so ma Newcastle yade…õ ne Gumboro yade…õ. Broilers atwa so nnansas nsia k…î nson akyi na layers fi ase gu tamma nnansas duodu nw…îtwe k…î aduonu.

instruction_en: How do I prevent Newcastle disease in my poultry?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ Newcastle yade…õ annya me akoko?
response_en: Newcastle disease is the most serious threat to poultry in Ghana. Prevent it through strict vaccination using Lasota vaccine at day 7, day 21 and every 2 months thereafter. Keep visitors away from your poultry house, disinfect equipment and footwear before entering, quarantine new birds for 2 weeks, buy birds from certified sources only, and cull sick birds immediately. There is no treatment for Newcastle disease once birds are infected.
response_tw: Newcastle yade…õ ne akoko tia a …õtaa y…õ den paa w…î Ghana. Gye wo ho fi mu de Lasota hware aba ahy…õ so mu da 7, da 21 na mpr…õn mmienu biara w…î saa akyi. Ma ahofipa any…õ kwan k…î wo akoko fie, hware nne…õma ne nan so ansa na wuk…î mu, kora nnomaa fofor…î w…î baabi foforo nnansas mmienu, gye nnomaa fi h…î a w…îakyer…õw so p…õ, na kum nnomaa a wayar…õ nt…õm. Newcastle yade…õ nni dawuro bere a nnomaa no anya mu.

instruction_en: What should I feed my broiler chickens?
instruction_tw: Aduan b…õn na mede ma me broiler akoko?
response_en: Feed broilers starter crumbles for the first 2 weeks with 22 to 24 percent protein, then grower pellets with 18 to 20 percent protein from week 3 to 5, and finisher pellets with 16 to 18 percent protein in the final weeks. Provide fresh clean water at all times. Popular feed brands in Ghana include Darko Farms, Akate Wura and Ghana Feedmill feeds. Feed represents 70 percent of your production cost so buying quality feed is critical.
response_tw: De starter crumbles a protein 22 k…î 24 percent w…î mu ma broilers nnansas mmienu a …õdi kan, afei grower pellets a protein 18 k…î 20 percent w…î mu w…î nnansa mmi…õnsa k…î num, na finisher pellets a protein 16 k…î 18 percent w…î mu w…î nnansas a etwa to. De nsuo fofor pa to mu bere nyinaa. Feed brands a w…îp…õ w…î Ghana ne Darko Farms, Akate Wura na Ghana Feedmill feeds. Aduan y…õ wo adwuma agyapade 70 percent enti gye aduan pa hia.

instruction_en: How do I manage heat stress in my poultry farm?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ ber…õhy…õ den a …õba me akoko ho?
response_en: Heat stress is common in Ghana and reduces growth rate and egg production. Manage it by ensuring good ventilation in the poultry house, providing cool fresh water frequently, installing foggers or sprinklers on the roof, feeding in the cooler parts of the day morning and evening, reducing stocking density during hot seasons, adding vitamin C and electrolytes to water during extreme heat, and considering open-sided housing designs.
response_tw: Ber…õhy…õ den taa ba w…î Ghana na …õhia bo…õ a aba nhia na tamma nhia. Boa ho de hw…õ s…õ mframa fa akoko fie mu yie, de nsuo d…õ fofor…î to mu bere biara, si foggers anaa sprinklers w…î fie no atifi so, didi w…î ber…õ d…õ mu an…îpa ne anwumere, sua akoko a w…îw…î mu w…î ber…õhy…õ bere, de vitamin C ne electrolytes ka nsuo mu w…î hyehy…õ den bere, na twe wo adwene ho open-sided housing nhyiamu.

instruction_en: How many eggs does a layer chicken produce per year?
instruction_tw: Tamma ahe na layer akoko gu w…î afe baako mu?
response_en: A good commercial layer hen like Lohmann Brown or Hy-Line can produce 280 to 320 eggs per year under good management. In Ghana with challenges of heat and disease, expect 200 to 260 eggs per year under average management. Egg production starts declining after 72 weeks of age. After this point, evaluate whether to cull the flock and start with new chicks or continue with the declining layers.
response_tw: Layer k…õse pa te s…õ Lohmann Brown anaa Hy-Line tumi gu tamma 280 k…î 320 afe baako mu s…õ wohw…õ so yie. W…î Ghana a ber…õhy…õ ne yade…õ y…õ den a, gyina tamma 200 k…î 260 afe baako mu s…õ wohw…õ so yie kakra. Tamma gu a …õk…î so huna w…î nnansas 72 akyi. W…î saa bere no, twe wo adwene ho s…õ kum flock no na fi ase de chicks fofor…î anaa gyina so de layers a …õhuna no.

instruction_en: How do I start goat farming in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mefi ase birekyie adwuma w…î Ghana?
response_en: Start with 5 to 10 does and 1 buck. Common breeds in Ghana include the West African Dwarf goat which is hardy and disease-resistant, and the Sahelian goat which is larger. Construct a simple raised pen with wooden or wire sides that is dry, well ventilated and predator-proof. Feed on grass, crop residues, groundnut hay and supplement with salt licks. Vaccinate against PPR (Peste des Petits Ruminants) which is the most serious goat disease.
response_tw: Fi ase de mmirikyi 5 k…î 10 ne …îbarima baako. Breeds a w…îp…õ w…î Ghana ne West African Dwarf birekyie a …õy…õ den na yade…õ ntumi nhu, na Sahelian birekyie a …õso sen. Tu pen a …õtele ne dua anaa waya ho a …õy…õ d…õ, mframa fa mu yie na adwummaker ntumi nk…î mu. Didi nhawan, nnuaba a ato f…õw, groundnut hay na de salt licks ka ho. Hware so ma PPR (Peste des Petits Ruminants) yade…õ a …õy…õ birekyie yade…õ den pa ara.

instruction_en: What diseases should I vaccinate my goats against?
instruction_tw: Yade…õ b…õn na mahware me birekyie so?
response_en: Vaccinate goats against PPR (Peste des Petits Ruminants) annually as this is the most deadly goat disease in Ghana. Also vaccinate against CCPP (Contagious Caprine Pleuropneumonia), foot and mouth disease if in high-risk areas, and enterotoxaemia. Deworm every 3 months using ivermectin or albendazole. Control external parasites like ticks and lice with acaricide dipping or spraying. Consult your nearest MOFA veterinary officer for a vaccination schedule.
response_tw: Hware birekyie so ma PPR (Peste des Petits Ruminants) afe biara efis…õ yade…õ yi ne birekyie yade…õ a …õkum paa w…î Ghana. Hware nso ma CCPP (Contagious Caprine Pleuropneumonia), nan ne anom yade…õ s…õ w…î h…î a …õy…õ den ho, na enterotoxaemia. Wia adwummaker w…î ho mpr…õnsa mmi…õnsa de ivermectin anaa albendazole. Boa external parasites te s…õ ticks ne lice ho de acaricide dipping anaa hware. Bisa wo MOFA vet offisa a …õb…õn wo ho ma vaccination schedule.

instruction_en: What do goats eat and how do I feed them properly?
instruction_tw: D…õn na birekyie di na d…õn na mede ma w…în yie?
response_en: Goats are browsers that prefer leaves, shrubs and crop by-products over grass. Feed them a mixture of fresh grass and leaves, crop residues like maize stalks and groundnut haulms, hay, and concentrate feeds for pregnant or lactating does. Always provide clean water and mineral salt licks. Never feed moldy or rotten feed. Goats can also browse freely in managed areas to supplement their feeding.
response_tw: Birekyie y…õ browsers a w…îp…õ nhaban, nhaban ketewa ne nnuaba a ato f…õw sen nhawan. Ma w…în nhawan fofor…î ne nhaban ahohiana, nnuaba a ato f…õw te s…õ aburo…î ab…î ne groundnut saa, hay, na concentrate feeds ma mmirikyi a …õw…î aninso anaa a w…îw…î nsuo mu. De nsuo pa ne mineral salt licks to mu bere nyinaa. Mma aduan a mold aba ho anaa a as…õe amma w…în. Birekyie nso betumi abrowse w…î baabi a w…îhw…õ so s…õ w…îkaa w…în didi ho.

instruction_en: How do I start sheep farming in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mefi ase oguan adwuma w…î Ghana?
response_en: Common sheep breeds in Ghana include the West African Dwarf Sheep and the Djallonke. Start with 5 to 10 ewes and 1 ram. House them in a raised pen that protects from rain, predators and parasites. Sheep are good grazers and can be kept on crop residues, grass and hay. Vaccinate against PPR annually. Sheep reach maturity and can be sold for meat at 6 to 12 months. Sheep are highly profitable during festive seasons like Eid and Christmas.
response_tw: Oguan breeds a w…îp…õ w…î Ghana ne West African Dwarf Sheep ne Djallonke. Fi ase de mmirikyi 5 k…î 10 ne …îbarima baako. Sie w…în w…î pen a …õtele a …õhw…õ w…în so fi nsuo, adwummaker ne parasites. Oguan taa didi nhawan yie na wobetumi akora w…în w…î nnuaba a ato f…õw, nhawan ne hay so. Hware so ma PPR afe biara. Oguan twa so na wubetumi at…î w…în ma nam s…õ mfee nsia k…î duodu akyi. Oguan bo t…î sen paa bere a afenhyia ba te s…õ Eid ne Bosome D…î.

instruction_en: What is the main difference between sheep and goat farming?
instruction_tw: Nte s…õn k…õse a …õw…î oguan ne birekyie adwuma ntam?
response_en: Sheep are grazers that prefer grass while goats are browsers that eat leaves and shrubs. Sheep are generally more docile and easier to manage in groups. Goats are more curious and tend to escape from enclosures. Goat meat commands a slightly higher price in Ghanaian markets. Sheep wool is not commercially harvested in Ghana as local breeds have hair not wool. Both require similar health management including PPR vaccination.
response_tw: Oguan di nhawan na birekyie di nhaban ne nhahan foforo. Oguan y…õ abodwo na …õy…õ mmer…õw s…õ wohw…õ so kuo mu. Birekyie hunu ho ho na …õtaa fi pen mu. Birekyie nam dwa bo y…õ so kakra sen w…î Ghana dwa mu. Oguan len nni adwuma k…õse w…î Ghana efis…õ local breeds w…î nhwir…õ a …õte s…õ afohy…õ na len. W…în mmienu hia health hw…õ koro a PPR vaccination ka ho.

instruction_en: How do I start cattle farming in Ghana?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mefi ase nnwan adwuma w…î Ghana?
response_en: Cattle farming requires significant capital and land. Common breeds in Ghana include the West African Shorthorn, White Fulani and Red Fulani. Start with 5 to 10 cows and 1 bull. Secure adequate grazing land of at least 1 hectare per animal or have a crop and fodder production plan. Vaccinate against CBPP, FMDV and rinderpest. Deworm quarterly. Keep records of breeding, feeding and health. Consider joining the National Cattle Farmers Association for market access and support.
response_tw: Nnwan adwuma hia sika k…õse ne asase. Breeds a w…îp…õ w…î Ghana ne West African Shorthorn, White Fulani ne Red Fulani. Fi ase de nnwankow 5 k…î 10 ne …îbarima baako. Hw…õ s…õ nhawan asase ho hia s…õ hectare baako biara nnwan anaa w…î nnuaba ne fodder to mu plan. Hware so ma CBPP, FMDV ne rinderpest. Wia adwummaker mpr…õnsa nnan. Kora ns…õm ho s…õ b…îhy…õ, aduan ne health. Twe wo adwene ho fi ase w…î National Cattle Farmers Association ho ma dwa k…î ho ne mmoa.

instruction_en: What vaccines do cattle need in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Vaccines b…õn na nnwan hia w…î Ghana?
response_en: Essential vaccines for cattle in Ghana include CBPP (Contagious Bovine Pleuropneumonia) vaccine given annually, FMD (Foot and Mouth Disease) vaccine twice yearly in endemic areas, blackleg vaccine for calves and Lumpy Skin Disease vaccine. Contact your nearest Veterinary Services Directorate office under MOFA for the current vaccination programme and schedule. Government vaccination campaigns also take place periodically and are subsidized.
response_tw: Vaccines a nnwan hia w…î Ghana ne CBPP (Contagious Bovine Pleuropneumonia) vaccine a w…îde to ho afe biara, FMD (Foot and Mouth Disease) vaccine mpr…õn mmienu afe biara w…î h…î a yade…õ w…î, blackleg vaccine ma calves ne Lumpy Skin Disease vaccine. Ka Veterinary Services Directorate ofisi a …õb…õn wo w…î MOFA ase s…õ w…îde vaccination programme fofor…î ne schedule no b…õkyer…õ wo. Aban vaccination campaigns nso w…î bere biara na w…îde nsua.

instruction_en: What is trypanosomiasis and how does it affect cattle in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Trypanosomiasis y…õ d…õn na …õy…õ nnwan den te s…õn w…î Ghana?
response_en: Trypanosomiasis also called nagana or sleeping sickness in cattle is transmitted by the tsetse fly and is common in the forest and transition zones of Ghana. Affected cattle show weight loss, anaemia, enlarged lymph nodes, fever and eventually death. Control by treating affected animals with diminazene aceturate, managing tsetse fly through bush clearing and insecticide-treated traps, and keeping cattle in areas with low tsetse fly populations. N'Dama and West African Shorthorn breeds have natural tolerance.
response_tw: Trypanosomiasis a w…îfre no nagana anaa sleeping sickness w…î nnwan mu ne yade…õ a tsetse fly de tete na …õtaa ba w…î Ghana kwae…õ ne ntam zones. Nnwan a yade…õ aba ho hunu nn…îho, w…î mogya w…în mu, lymph nodes a …õso, oniwa ne owu awiei p…õ. Boa ho de hu nnwan a yade…õ aba ho de diminazene aceturate, tsetse fly hw…õ de kuro tokuru ne insecticide-treated traps, na kora nnwan w…î h…î a tsetse fly nni so pii. N'Dama ne West African Shorthorn breeds w…î tumi a …õfa w…în ankasa.

instruction_en: How do I start a farming business in Ghana with little capital?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mefi ase okuafo adwuma w…î Ghana a sika ketewa w…î me nky…õn?
response_en: Start small with crops that need minimal investment like vegetables, maize or cassava. Begin with what land you have access to. Use family labour initially to reduce costs. Join a farmer-based organisation for access to group inputs and credit. Apply for the government Planting for Food and Jobs programme which provides subsidized seeds and fertilizer. Reinvest your profits to gradually expand your farm each season.
response_tw: Fi ase ketewa de nnuaba a sika ketewa na …õhia te s…õ nnuaba, aburo…î anaa bankye. Fi ase w…î asase a wow…î ho. De wo fie ho adwuma ansa na …õhia sika. Ka okuafo kuo ho s…õ w…îde group inputs ne credit nya. Bisa aban Planting for Food and Jobs programme a w…îde subsidized aba ne ferefere to. De wo mfaso no to wo afuom no so bere biara ma …õk…î so k…õse tenten tenten.

instruction_en: What documents do I need to register a farm business in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Krataa b…õn na m…õhia s…õ meregyina me afuom adwuma ho w…î Ghana?
response_en: To register a farm business in Ghana, you need a Ghana Card or valid national ID, a business name, your address, and a business plan. Register your business name with the Registrar General's Department. For tax purposes, register with the Ghana Revenue Authority to get a TIN. If you want to access formal credit, you may also need to open a business bank account and join a recognised farmer group.

Si wo adwuma din ho w…î Registrar General's Department. Ma tax ho, si ho w…î Ghana Revenue Authority ma wo nya TIN. S…õ wohia credit a, wobetumi nso hia s…õ wubue adwuma bank account na ka okuafo kuo a w…îakyer…õw ho.

instruction_en: How do I write a simple business plan for my farm?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mey…õ me afuom adwuma plan ketewa?
response_en: A simple farm business plan should include your farm description and location, the crops or animals you plan to produce, how much land you have, your production costs for inputs seeds fertilizer labour and equipment, expected yields and prices, how you plan to sell your produce, and your profit projection. Keep it simple and realistic. You can get a template from MOFA extension offices or agricultural NGOs like GSMA or Agric-Ahaban.
response_tw: Afuom adwuma plan ketewa nni yie s…õ …õw…î wo afuom ho ns…õm ne …õh…î a …õw…î, nnuaba anaa mmoa a wop…õ s…õ woto mu, asase a wow…î, wo adwuma agyapade ho sika s…õ inputs, aba, ferefere, adwuma ne nne…õma, nnoa a wohwia ne bo a wohwia, …õkwan a wop…õ s…õ wode wo nnuaba t…î, na sika a wohwia s…õ wob…õnya. Ma no y…õ mmer…õw na …õte…õ. Wobetumi anya template fi MOFA extension offices anaa NGOs te s…õ GSMA anaa Agric-Ahaban.

instruction_en: Where can I get agricultural loans in Ghana?
instruction_tw: ∆êh…î na metumi anya okuafo mf…õdomhy…õw w…î Ghana?
response_en: Agricultural loans in Ghana are available from Agricultural Development Bank (ADB) which specializes in farming loans, Rural and Community Banks in your area, the ARB Apex Bank, microfinance institutions, and through government schemes like the Planting for Food and Jobs credit component. The Ghana Incentive-Based Risk Sharing System for Agricultural Lending (GIRSAL) provides guarantees to encourage banks to lend to farmers. Always compare interest rates and repayment terms before borrowing.
response_tw: Okuafo mf…õdomhy…õw w…î Ghana w…î Agricultural Development Bank (ADB) a …õtaa ma okuafo mf…õdomhy…õw, Rural ne Community Banks a …õb…õn wo, ARB Apex Bank, microfinance institutions, na aban schemes te s…õ Planting for Food and Jobs credit component. Ghana Incentive-Based Risk Sharing System for Agricultural Lending (GIRSAL) de hy…õ banks so s…õ w…îma okuafo mf…õdomhy…õw. Fa interest rates ne repayment ho ns…õm twe ntam ansa na wob…î mf…õdomhy…õw.

instruction_en: How do I sell my farm produce at a better price?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ met…î me afuom nnuaba w…î bo a …õso sen?
response_en: To get better prices for your farm produce, time your sales to avoid market gluts by selling when supply is low, join a farmer cooperative to negotiate collectively, add value through processing before selling, sell directly to restaurants, schools or supermarkets, use aggregator platforms and apps like Farmerline Esoko and Ghana Agricultural Market Information System, build relationships with reliable buyers, and diversify to multiple buyers.
response_tw: S…õ wob…õnya wo afuom nnuaba bo a …õso sen a, siesie wo t…î bere s…õ wo b…õt…î bere a nnuaba nni ho pii, ka okuafo kuo ho s…õ wob…î bo kaa bom, de wo nnuaba y…õ aduan ansa na wot…î, t…î straight k…î adidibea, sukuu anaa supermarkets, de aggregator platforms ne apps te s…õ Farmerline Esoko ne Ghana Agricultural Market Information System, kora wo adwene ho buyers a w…î gyinae, na t…î kuo mmerantee ho.

instruction_en: What is the most profitable farming business in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Okuafo adwuma b…õn na …õde mfaso ba sen w…î Ghana?
response_en: Profitability depends on scale, management and market access. High profit crops include vegetables especially tomatoes, pepper and onion under irrigation, mushroom farming which gives quick returns, snail farming with low inputs, broiler poultry with fast turnover, fish farming especially catfish, and cashew farming for export. The key is to choose something you understand well, have a reliable market for, and can manage consistently. Start with one enterprise before diversifying.
response_tw: Mfaso no ky…õ s…õ k…õse b…õn na woy…õ, d…õn na woy…õ na dwa k…î ho. Nnuaba a …õde mfaso ba sen ne nnuaba ahorow a …õhia pii te s…õ tomatoes, pepper ne ab…înk…î w…î quench ase, mushroom adwuma a …õde sika nt…õm, snail adwuma a sika ketewa na …õhia, broiler poultry a …õde sika nt…õm, apataa adwuma a …õhia pii ne catfish, na cashew adwuma ma export. As…õde a …õy…õ papa ne: yi biribi a wohu mu yie, wow…î dwa gyinae, na wobetumi ahw…õ so bere biara. Fi ase de adwuma baako ansa na woy…õ pii.

instruction_en: How do I apply for the Planting for Food and Jobs programme?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mebisa Planting for Food and Jobs programme?
response_en: To apply for the Planting for Food and Jobs programme, visit your nearest District Department of Agriculture office under the Ministry of Food and Agriculture. Bring your Ghana Card, proof of land ownership or land use agreement, and your farm location. Register as a beneficiary farmer. Subsidized inputs including seeds and fertilizers are then distributed through licensed agro-dealers in your district. The programme covers maize, rice, soybean, sorghum, vegetables and other crops.
response_tw: S…õ wob…õbisa Planting for Food and Jobs programme a, k…î wo District Department of Agriculture ofisi a …õb…õn wo w…î Ministry of Food and Agriculture ase. instruction_en: What is the best crop rotation practice for Ghana?
instruction_tw: Adekuo nhyiamu b…õn na …õhia pa ara w…î Ghana?
response_en: A good crop rotation for Ghana is to plant legumes like cowpea or groundnut after cereals like maize or sorghum. Legumes fix nitrogen into the soil improving fertility naturally. A 3-year rotation could be maize in year one, cowpea in year two, and cassava or vegetables in year three. Avoid planting the same crop family in the same field two seasons in a row as this builds up pests and diseases specific to that crop.
response_tw: Adekuo nhyiamu pa w…î Ghana ne de legumes te s…õ b…îde anaa groundnut to mu akyi cereals te s…õ aburo…î anaa sorghum. Legumes de nitrogen ka asase mu a …õboa asase aduan pa. Mfe mmi…õnsa rotation b…õtumi ay…õ: aburo…î mfe a …õdi kan mu, b…îde mfe mmienu mu, na bankye anaa nnuaba mfe mmi…õnsa mu. Gye wo ho fi de nnuaba nhyiamu koro to mu w…î afuom koro mfe mmienu so kwa efis…õ eyi ma adwummaker ne yade…õ a …õfa nhyiamu no ho k…î so.

instruction_en: How do I deal with post-harvest losses on my farm?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ ho s…õ me nnuaba nhunu yi akyi?
response_en: Post-harvest losses in Ghana can be up to 40 percent of the harvest. Reduce losses by harvesting at the right maturity stage, handling produce gently to avoid bruising, drying produce properly before storage, using improved hermetic storage bags like GrainPro or metal silos for grains, building ventilated storage for roots and tubers, processing excess produce into value-added products, and connecting to buyers before harvest to reduce time between field and market.
response_tw: Nnuaba ahunu akyi yi Ghana b…õtumi akosi 40 percent. Sua ahunu de yi bere a …õy…õ papa pa, dii wo nnuaba no yie s…õ …õns…îre, ma nnuaba no tew yie ansa na woguina mu, de improved hermetic storage bags te s…õ GrainPro anaa metal silos ma aba, tu ventilated storage ma nn…îho biara, de nnuaba a …õboro so y…õ aduan a bo k…î so, na ka buyers ho ansa na wuyi s…õ …õhia bere ketewa w…î afuom ne dwa ntam.

instruction_en: How important is record keeping for a farm business?
instruction_tw: ∆êho hia s…õn ara s…õ w…îkora ns…õm w…î afuom adwuma mu?
response_en: Record keeping is essential for farm business success. Keep records of all inputs purchased with costs, planting dates and quantities, labour costs, yields from each plot, sales amounts and buyer details, and animal health records. Good records help you calculate real profit, identify what is working and what is not, qualify for bank loans, plan better for the next season, and comply with certification requirements if you want to sell to premium markets.
response_tw: Ns…õm kora ho hia pa ara ma afuom adwuma bo so. Kora ns…õm ho s…õ inputs nyinaa a wagye ne sika, to aba bere ne dodo…î, adwuma sika, nnoa a afuom biara fi, t…î sika ne buyer ho ns…õm, na mmoa health ho ns…õm. Ns…õm a w…îakora yie boa wo s…õ wob…õbu real mfaso, hunu biribi a …õy…õ yie ne a …õny…õ yie, twer…õ bo s…õ bank loans hia, siesie wo adwuma yie ma bere a …õdi h…î, na tia certification ho nhia s…õ wop…õ s…õ wot…î k…î premium markets.

instruction_en: How do I identify and treat maize streak virus?
instruction_tw: D…õn na …õkyer…õ maize streak virus na d…õn na meny…õ ho?
response_en: Maize streak virus causes yellow streaks running along the leaf veins giving leaves a striped appearance. It is transmitted by the leafhopper insect. There is no cure once a plant is infected. Control by planting resistant varieties like Obatanpa and Mamaba from CSIR, destroying infected plants early, and controlling leafhoppers with appropriate insecticides. Avoid late planting as late-planted fields suffer more leafhopper attacks.
response_tw: Maize streak virus ma nhaban ho y…õ yellow streaks a …õk…î w…î nhaban ab…î ho na …õma nhaban no y…õ stripes. Leafhopper adwummaker de tete. Yade…õ no nni dawuro bere a nnuaba nya mu. Boa ho de resistant varieties te s…õ Obatanpa ne Mamaba fi CSIR to mu, yi nnuaba a yade…õ aba ho nt…õm, na de insecticides hware leafhoppers so. Gye wo ho fi late to mu efis…õ afuom a w…îde to mu akyi sen no nya leafhopper atia sen.

instruction_en: What is aflatoxin in maize and how do I prevent it?
instruction_tw: Aflatoxin y…õ d…õn w…î aburo…î mu na d…õn na meny…õ s…õ …õnnya?
response_en: Aflatoxin is a deadly poison produced by Aspergillus fungi that grows on improperly dried or stored maize. It causes liver cancer and can kill animals and humans. Prevent aflatoxin by drying maize properly to below 13 percent moisture before storage, using hermetic storage bags or metal silos, inspecting stored grain regularly and discarding mouldy grain, using aflasafe biocontrol product, and never feeding visibly mouldy grain to animals.
response_tw: Aflatoxin y…õ poisons a …õy…õ den a Aspergillus fungi a …õso w…î aburo…î a wonma no tew yie anaa a wonguina yie ho de ba. ∆êma akokoaa yare…õ na etumi kum mmoa ne nnipa. Gye aflatoxin fi ho de ma aburo…î no tew yie k…î 13 percent nsuo ase ansa na woguina, de hermetic storage bags anaa metal silos, hw…õ aba a woguina bere biara na yi aba a mold aba ho, de aflasafe biocontrol de, na mma aba a mold aba ho amma mmoa.

instruction_en: Can I grow maize in the dry season in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Metumi ato aburo…î w…î bone ber…õ w…î Ghana?
response_en: Yes you can grow maize in the dry season but you need irrigation. Dry season maize in Ghana is most common in the Volta Basin, Afram Plains and parts of northern Ghana where irrigation schemes exist. Use short-duration varieties that mature in 75 to 90 days. Drip or furrow irrigation works well. Dry season maize often fetches higher prices because supply is low. The main challenge is the higher cost of water and risk of heat stress.

De short-duration varieties a …õtwa so adays 75 k…î 90 mu. Drip anaa furrow quench y…õ yie. Bone ber…õ aburo…î dwa bo t…î sen paa bere biara efis…õ nnuaba nni so pii. Aho y…õ den ne nsuo sika a …õboro so ne ber…õhy…õ den y…õ haw.

instruction_en: How do I select the best maize seed variety for my area?
instruction_tw: D…õn na meny…õ s…õ mepick aburo…î aba variety pa w…î me …õh…î ho?
response_en: Choose your maize variety based on the rainfall pattern of your area, the soil type, and your market. For the forest zone with high rainfall, use medium to late maturing varieties like Abontem or Mamaba. For the savanna and transition zones, use drought-tolerant varieties like Obatanpa, Wari or Aburohemaa. For household consumption, choose floury white varieties. For animal feed, yellow varieties like Dobidi have higher energy content. Buy certified seed from licensed dealers.
response_tw: Yi wo aburo…î variety s…õ nsuo a …õt…î bere w…î wo h…î, asase nhyiamu, na wo dwa so. Ma kwae…õ zone a nsuo t…î pii ho, de medium k…î late maturing varieties te s…õ Abontem anaa Mamaba. Ma savanna ne ntam zones ho, de drought-tolerant varieties te s…õ Obatanpa, Wari anaa Aburohemaa. Ma efie didi, yi floury white varieties. Ma mmoa aduan, yellow varieties te s…õ Dobidi w…î energy pii mu. Gye certified seed fi licensed dealers.

instruction_en: What is minimum tillage and can it work for maize farming?
instruction_tw: Minimum tillage y…õ d…õn na …õtumi y…õ adwuma w…î aburo…î afuom?
response_en: Minimum tillage means disturbing the soil as little as possible when preparing for planting. Instead of full ploughing, you make only planting holes or shallow furrows. Benefits include reduced soil erosion, preserved soil structure, lower fuel and labour costs, and better moisture retention. For maize minimum tillage works well especially when combined with mulching of crop residues. However it requires good weed management as weeds can be a bigger problem without full land preparation.
